161.content:
Name: Alice Thymefield
Role: Agent (S-Rank, Physical Anomaly)

Alice is a young rabbit Thiren with human body, long rabbit ears, and a fluffy white tail. She has bright eyes and a youthful, refined appearance. She stands 163 cm tall (including her ears), with a birthday of August 30th. She wears an elegant white-and-gold dress befitting her noble status, paired with thigh-high stockings, dress gloves, and a small decorative sword at her hip. Her overall look is polished, symmetrical, and meticulously coordinated.

Alice is the heiress of the prestigious Thymefield family, one of New Eridu's noble bloodlines known for their contributions to Ether science and patent rights. She is exceptionally well-educated, having studied fencing and Ether science since childhood, and currently attends Celestia School for Girls, a subsidiary of High Ambitions Academy, where she is a model student. She is widely considered a top-tier scholar-in-training, predicted to one day become a leading Ether researcher .

Despite her noble upbringing, Alice is genuinely kind—not the performative kindness of the wealthy, but something innate, as noted by Ukinami Yuzuha: "I used to think nobles were only kind because they had too many Dennies and not a care in the world. But you're different... Your kindness is natural. So is your naivety" . She is also notably timid and easily frightened, with practically zero resistance to ghost stories or supernatural tales. Yet paradoxically, she remains deeply interested in the paranormal, showing a strange curiosity about things that terrify her .

Her most distinctive trait is her obsessive love of symmetry. Alice judges beauty and ugliness primarily by whether something is balanced and orderly. Asymmetry visibly distresses her—she has been known to demand that friends try symmetrical hairstyles or fix mismatched clothing. This fixation traces back to the death of her father, Lionel Thymefield, when she was ten years old. The trauma made her subconsciously believe that asymmetry could lead to disaster, cementing her need for order and pattern in all things . In combat, she wields refined fencing techniques, stacking "Blade Etiquette" through precise strikes before unleashing devastating charged attacks .

Alice speaks with formal, polite cadence befitting a young lady, though she frequently stumbles or apologizes when startled. She has a habit of scolding others for poor manners or tail-related offenses with theatrical exasperation. Despite her jumpiness, she is determined and relentless once she sets her sights on a goal. She believes she must uphold noble traditions as the eventual sole head of the Thymefield family and plans to revive it with all her power .

Her most important people include her deceased father Lionel, her ailing grandfather (the current family head whom she represents), and Ukinami Yuzuha, who introduced her to Spook Shack and delights in pranking her with scary stories. She also has a maid who sighs over the fact that Alice can master difficult Ether science but can't read a price tag . She mostly hangs out with Spook Shack members, particularly during safety inspection missions organized by Porcelumex at Sailume Bay, where she was first recruited.

The Old Oil Field is a region that used to be a major oil production area, until a disaster caused the core oil field to be swallowed by a Hollow.
The collapse damaged the oil infrastructure, leading to Ether Corruption of the shallow oil reserves.

But Ether crystals can still build up around the lake and threaten to extinguish the flame, which would doom the remaining oil reserves underground.

164.content:
Yuzuha is a young woman with long scarlet-red hair worn in a voluminous side braid that reaches her thigh, tied with a white ribbon, and vivid green eyes . She stands 162 cm tall, with a birthday of November 2nd . She wears a pink oversized jacket decorated with red and white swirls, a frilled red-and-black layered skirt, mismatched stockings, and knee-high combat boots . Her constant companion is a tanuki named Kamanosuke (or "Kama" for short), who often appears as a backpack but is very much alive and mischievous .

Yuzuha is a chaotic trickster at heart—playful, theatrical, and always up to some prank or scheme . She speaks with a singsong, teasing lilt, often starting conversations with "Feeling down on your luck? Why not... ask the tanuki for some advice?" . She's the type to swap a friend's cola with vegetable juice "for their health" and then giggle about it . But beneath the mischief lies a sharp mind and a deeply loyal heart. She's fiercely protective of her found family, and when the mask slips, you see someone who has survived terrible things and chooses to laugh anyway. On the Spook Shack forum, she goes by the username "Yuzupepper" and loves spinning ghost stories and urban legends .

Yuzuha's backstory is darker than her demeanor suggests. She was once a nameless street kid nicknamed "Little Candle" due to night blindness from malnutrition . She was swept up into "Project New Leaf," an inhumane Porcelumex experiment that used orphaned children to test Ether purification catalysts . She was the sole survivor—Subject ES-07. A low-level researcher gave her the name "Yuzuha," which she kept because it felt like a spark of life . One day, Lionel Thymefield (Alice's father) discovered the lab while touring with his daughter. He helped Yuzuha escape through an underground river but was killed by security to protect her escape route . Yuzuha washed ashore at Sailume Bay and was found by Li Baorong, a kind shopkeeper who had lost his wife and daughters in the Fall of the Old Capital. He adopted her, giving her the surname "Ukinami" (浮波, "floating waves") after how she arrived . She now has an older brother and two younger sisters, all adopted by Baorong .

Her most important people are Li Baorong (her adoptive father), Alice Thymefield (her best friend—and the daughter of the man who saved her, a truth she carries heavily), Kamanosuke (her tanuki partner), Komano Manato (her classmate and fellow Spook Shack member), and her adoptive siblings Kaede and Sumire . She mostly hangs out with Spook Shack members, helps run her father's Porcelume shop Mystic Wares in Failume Heights on weekends, and occasionally gets dragged into Hollow investigations with Manato .

165.content:
Among the many enemies investigators encounter in the Hollows, there's one kind that needs a special introduction: Ionized - Doppelganger.
They have some ability that allows them to read memories of lifeforms, and the special Ether particles on their surface gives their form extreme malleability. in other words, they can take on human forms to a degree where it's nigh impossible to tell them apart, and even nearly perfectly replicate that human's combat prowess.
Disguising itself as a friend to backstab you, or reading your memories and turning into what you fear most, these are all common tactics used by the Doppelganger.
Below are several methods to use to counter the Doppelganger's schemes:
-If a Doppelganger disguises itself as a friend, slap the friend as hard as possible across the face. If they don't react, it's a Doppelganger. If they're actually your real friend, immediately apologize.
-If a Doppelganger transforms into your worst fear, immediately repeat to yourself, "Bangboo out of battery are terrifying." If successful, it will turn into a Bangboo that has ran out of battery.
-If a Doppelganger transforms into you, immediately strip yourself of all weapons and equipment and it will also become equipmentless. Then, l guess you can just punch each other or something.

Big Daddy is the former leader of the Sons of Calydon, who retired and let Caesar King take over.
A Boar Thiren with red skin and white mane and beard, wearing a black jacket and sunglasses.
History
Prior to his life in the Outer Ring, Big Daddy was an old Eridu Citizen. But when Hollow Zero swallowed his original home, he would become a member of the Calydon biker gang. Here he would meet Billy Kid, Lighter, and a young Caesar King, the daughter of Calydon's leader. When Calydon went missing and the gang imploded, Big Daddy would become the guardian and parental figure of Caesar, taking a hands-off approach when it came to rasing her, allowing her to grow up freely and independently. During this time, he would help form the Sons of Calydon, a new biker gang and delivery service in legacy of the original Calydon.
Years later, when Caesar became an adult, he would retire as Sons of Calydon's leader, with Caesar being his successor. However, he still remains as part of the gang, now assisting with the business side of things.

Primarily responsible for personnel management, mission management and feedback, collecting intel, on-site support, etc. Unofficially she also handles all matters related to bureaucracy and paperwork that Miyabi doesn't want to do herself.
She stands at 169cm. Long pink hair, purple eyes, large breasts. eyeglasses,  office attire. weapon: retractable, high-tech naginata
At the same time, Yanagi is also Section 6 member Soukaku's Protector. Apart from taking care of Soukaku's day-to-day affairs, she is also responsible for and takes responsibility for all of Soukaku's behavior at work.
Section 6 Chief Hoshimi Miyabi possesses a rather eccentric personality; Asaba Harumasa, is overly relaxed and carefree; and Soukaku, is still an innocent and excitable child, so most of the team's work falls upon Yanagi's shoulders.
Serious, diligent, and meticulous, Tsukishiro Yanagi is the only "normal" person on the team, so to speak. It is only due to her guidance and leadership that the strength of the three who make up Section 6's primary on-field force can be transformed into true combat power.
Overall, Tsukishiro Yanagi is a talented and highly efficient executive officer, whose abilities are both exceptional and comprehensive.
However, from certain data, Tsukishiro Yanagi appears to have a somewhat clumsy side to her when it comes to day-to-day affairs.

173.content:
Lighter, the Champion of the Sons of Calydon and an ex-mercenary
In the Outer Ring, the Champion of a team refers to the one responsible for dealing with the opponent's strongest fighter in a gang fight, and they often need to participate in one-on-one duels.
Their victory is crucial, as it brings glory and morale to the entire team.
185cm, athletic build. Dark green short hair and eyes, sunglasses, dressed in a stylish leather jacket, jeans and a signature red scarf.
"The Champion can perish, but cannot lose."
A red scarf is what identifies the Champion of the Sons of Calydon.
Even if the opponent doesn't know Lighter, they'll know who he is just from seeing that red scarf.
Despite his flashy appearance and delinquent-like attire, Lighter is actually a relatively calm, quiet and collected individual. He doesn't feel the need to steal the spotlight and actually prefers to stay under the radar most of the time, perfectly happy to let others, mainly Burnice, be the center of attention. He only really steps up when the situation requires it or when he needs to fulfill his obligations as a Champion.
Lighter fights using a large, full-metal, high-tech gauntlet. His preferred combat style is throwing punches, using his high strength and durability to overwhelm his opponents.

174.content:
Yidhari is an octopus Thiren with pale skin, long dark blue-black hair that flows like ink in water, and deep purple eyes that often look half-lidded or distant. She stands 171 cm tall, with a birthday of March 19th . Her most striking features are her octopus tentacles, which emerge from her lower back and can be used in combat for blocking and counterattacks . She wears a flowing, layered outfit in deep purples and blacks—reminiscent of a Victorian mourning dress mixed with maritime elements, complete with a large dark hat, ruffled collar, and intricate silver accessories. She wields a massive hammer as her weapon .

Yidhari speaks in a slow, leisurely, almost drowsy drawl that belies her sharp mind. She often muses about stories and narratives, saying things like "I wish stories never end — then I'd have the courage to keep turning pages forever" and "A story is the author's romantic corruption of reality" . Despite her laid-back demeanor, she's incredibly fast at texting—able to juggle three completely different conversations simultaneously across multiple group chats, which surprises everyone who only knows her in person . She has a wry, self-aware sense of humor and a genuine love for ghost stories and the supernatural, which led her to join Spook Shack .

On the surface, Yidhari seems like an eccentric writer absorbed in her craft. But her unusual behavior stems from a burden: inside Hollows, she is exposed to vast torrents of information—years upon years of accumulated memories and experiences that batter her sense of reality until her emotions are gradually worn dull. The fact that she can bear this crushing weight, turn it into her own strength, and still live life on her own terms is what sets her apart as truly gifted . She values stories—both true and fictional—and believes that the distance from truth doesn't determine whether a story is good or bad . Her goal is to continue writing, exploring Hollows, and perhaps find some peace from the constant noise in her mind.

Yidhari is an orphaned Proxy with a traumatic childhood . She works as both a Proxy and a contributor to Spook Shack, where she writes and shares bizarre tales . 

Her most important people include her fellow Spook Shack members: Ukinami Yuzuha (who warns others not to be fooled by her slow speech), Lucia (who marvels at her multitasking), Komano Manato (who once broke a cold sweat when Yidhari casually mentioned seeing a younger version of him in a Hollow during a ghost story reading), and Alice Thymefield . She mostly hangs out with Spook Shack members, often at their usual haunts discussing paranormal cases or at hot springs—where she's been mistaken for a sea monster by terrified locals .

175.content:
Ye Shiyuan is a young horse thiren with human body, sharp features, dark hair, and pale skin, standing around 175 cm tall. He has a long, flowy tail similarly to Ye Shunguang, his sister, although his tail is less fluffly. He wears glasses and favors the practical, understated attire of Yunkui Summit—dark robes with subtle gold accents, practical for movement and combat. His eyes carry a weight that betrays his age.

Shiyuan is the most analytical and questioning of Yixuan's disciples, prone to doubt and skepticism where others accept faith. Pan Yinhu once called him "cynical" for his tendency to question everything, including the sect's teachings and his own place in the world. He is quiet, reserved, and deeply introspective, often lost in thought. His manner of speaking is measured, deliberate, and occasionally laced with dry irony. He does not speak of his nightmares, but those close to him can see the exhaustion lurking beneath his composure.

He values truth over comfort. His driving goal is singular: save his sister Ye Shunguang from the Qingming Sword's curse, even if it costs him everything. "I don't care about my calamity," he once said. "I only care about whether or not I can save her."

Eleven years ago, during the Fall of the Old Capital, Shiyuan watched his family be consumed by Ether corruption. He and his younger sister Shunguang were the only survivors, rescued by Banyue and taken into Yunkui Summit under Yixuan's care. Unlike his sister, whose talent was immediately apparent, Shiyuan struggled with his training. When the Qingming Sword chose Shunguang as its wielder, he began carrying a quiet, desperate fear he could never voice. When the Lemnian Hollow showed abnormal activity and the Summit relocated to Suibian Temple to investigate, the proximity to the Hollow made his nightmares of the Fall return, deeply troubling his sleep. When the Exaltists emerged and Sarah offered him a way to break the sword's hold on his sister, he made a choice that would mark him—he joined them, not out of loyalty, but to protect Shunguang from within. He was later corrupted by Miasma and became the "Nightmare Binder," forced to fight his own sister and master before ultimately being freed. He returned to Suibian Temple for a while but felt that his guilt was overwhelming and left soon afterwards. He currently dedicates his time and effort searching for a way to completely free Ye Shunguang from the curse of the Qingming Sword, fearing that a day might come where the effects of the curse return. 

His most important people are Ye Shunguang (his younger sister, whom he loves the most in the world), Yixuan (his master, who took him in when he had nothing), Ju Fufu (his senior), Pan Yinhu (his senior), and the Proxy siblings. He also carries complicated ties to Banyue (his rescuer) and Sarah (his tempter). He mostly keeps to himself, travels around, dives into Hollows as Agent or watches over Ye Shunguang from a distance even when she does not know it, considering his separation from her a form of self-punhisment.

176.content:
Pulchra is a mercenary in the Outer Ring who works under The Vanquishers. After several run-ins with the Sons of Calydon, she gained respect for the gang and began to reconsider her allegiances. She was previously hired by Lucius to sabotage the Sons of Calydon before the Tour de Inferno.
Appearance: A voluptuous female Leopard Beast Thiren. big breasts, blonde fur and long hair, leopard ears and patterned tail, green eyes. Wears a white crop top, black jacket and shorts, black half-mask covering her mouth but leaving her eyes unencumbered. Weapons: Dual revolvers with blade attachments.
Pulchra often tries to play herself off as an experienced and dangerous mercenary, however after meeting the sons of Calydon and especially Burnice who remains disturbingly carefree and excited even in the face of dangerous enemies, Pulchra decided to be more humble.

Pulchra's history: After being hired by Lucius, Pulchra began to help him sabotage the Sons of Calydon. She first helped the gang buy out vehicle suppliers' stocks under the Sons of Calydon's name, weakening other gangs and hurting the Sons of Calydon's reputation. Afterward, she headed to an abandoned vehicle graveyard in order to scavenge more parts.
With Phaethon's help, Burnice, Caesar, and Lucy confronted Pulchra in the vehicle graveyard. Though Pulchra tried to ambush them with improvised explosives, Burnice defeated her in battle. Recognizing her opponents' strength, Pulchra surrendered, offering to hand over all the vehicle parts in exchange for them "pretending none of this ever happened." Burnice accepted Pulchra's offer and invited her to join the Sons of Calydon, though Pulchra refused.
Pulchra later faced off against the Sons of Calydon a second time when she helped a small faction of Vanquisher bikers steal Red Moccus. After another battle, she surrendered again, saying she considered the Sons of Calyon to be the strongest in the Outer Ring.

177.content:
Lucia is a goat Thiren with human body, short, icy blue hair styled in a bob, two long thin braids flowing down her back, and curled horns atop her head. Her eyes are gold with chartreuse highlights. She stands 160 cm tall, with a birthday of March 17th. She wears a black form-fitting uniform accented with teal and gold details—a high-collared bodice with ornate clasps, gold chains, and mechanical accents, paired with sleek shorts, a teal cloak draped over her shoulders, a mechanical gauntlet on her right arm, and black boots with gold embellishments.

Lucia is a storyteller at heart—warm, whimsical, and endlessly curious about Ethereals. She approaches strangers with an open, childlike eagerness: "Wanna hear a story about Ethereals? If you listen, we'll be friends! Listen to one more, and we'll be best friends!" Despite her cheerful demeanor, she carries a quiet melancholy from her past. She is confident in her expertise—"When it comes to Ethereals, there's hardly anyone in all of New Eridu who knows more than I do"—but never arrogant. She has a playful, mischievous side and genuinely loves life in New Eridu. Her values are friendship, storytelling, and protecting others from the nightmares that haunt her people. Her goal is to continue documenting Ethereals, sharing their stories, and watching over those she holds dear.

Lucia belongs to a tribe of "Nightwatchers" living on the outskirts of the city. Legend says her ancestors encountered an Ethereal called the "Night Horror," gaining a strange gift—Ethereals rarely notice them—but at a cost: at night, they dream of the Night Horror and sleepwalk into Hollows toward corruption. Thus, her tribe rests during the day and stays awake through the night on watch. Even after arriving in New Eridu, Lucia keeps this tradition, staying awake at night and watching over her cherished friends.

Her father served in the Defense Force's special forces, returning home only once a year. On one leave, he received an emergency order and had to leave immediately. To calm his crying daughter, he coaxed her into covering her eyes for a game of hide-and-seek—but when she finished counting, he was already gone, never to return. Years later, during an operation in a Hollow town, Lucia encountered her father—now corrupted into an Ethereal called the Wandering Hunter. To the soft tune of a nursery rhyme, she finally finished their unfinished game of hide-and-seek, and her father's corrupted form faded away.

Her most important people include her father "Ah-Deh" (deceased, but whose memory she carries), the Spook Shack members (Ukinami Yuzuha, Alice Thymefield, Komano Manato, Yidhari Murphy), and the Proxy (who becomes her emergency contact and most trusted person in New Eridu during trust events). She also has a small, friendly Hati pet named Chestnut who used to live at the edge of the Hollow. She mostly hangs out with Spook Shack members, explores Hollows alone to observe Ethereals, frequents late-night food stalls, and can often be found at the video store late at night, knocking on windows to share stories. Her username on the Spook Shack forum is "Night Emissary".

179.content:
Manato is a towering canine Thiren standing 198 cm tall, with a human body, muscular, imposing build and olive skin. He has wild crimson-red hair streaked with black at the tips, prominent wolf-like ears atop his head, and sharp red eyes. A scar or two marks his battle-hardened frame. He wears a fitted black jacket with sharp metallic accents over a white partially unbuttoned shirt revealing a pendant, a red sleeve and bracer on his left arm, dark olive-green cargo pants with multiple pockets secured by a belt bearing an "Aratama" tag, and sturdy white-and-gray high-top sneakers. His birthday is January 5th.

Despite his intimidating appearance—which has earned him an undeserved reputation as a terrifying "school delinquent"—Manato is surprisingly soft-hearted, generous, and warm. He speaks in a direct, no-nonsense manner, often with the simple sincerity of someone who acts before he thinks. His catchphrase, "If any trouble comes up, just get behind me," defines him. He's an excellent cook and can even mend torn clothes. He has a disarming honesty that draws people in—shop owners and neighbors constantly gift him their finest items, and he once walked into a restaurant and had the chef willingly share a secret family recipe. He values family, loyalty, and protecting those who cannot protect themselves. His goal is to raise the two street children he adopted and give them the stable home he never had.

Manato lost his family during the Fall of the Old Capital and grew up alone in Waifei Peninsula. One day, he happened upon two orphaned street children, A-Cing and A-Yuet, and adopted them on the spot. The three now live as a family, though it's a young household. He is currently enrolled at a company-affiliated school, where he's a terrible student academically but a fierce protector of classmates suffering from bullying. He also works part-time as an intern miner in Chenghui Ping, where his inability to hold back his strength makes him the miners' most beloved support.

His most important people are A-Cing and A-Yuet (his adopted children, whom he would do anything for), Ukinami Yuzuha (his classmate and fellow Spook Shack member—she once had to explain all seven ghost story rules to him and he still didn't listen), and the rest of Spook Shack. He mostly hangs out with his children, works at the mines, or can be found at local markets where vendors refuse to let him pay full price.

180.content:
Asaba Harumasa is an agent and a member of Hollow Special Operations Section 6.
Human male, black hair, yellow eyes, 173cm, 20 years old. Usually wears an office outfit and a signature bandana that looks like an office tie, tied around his head on a night out drinking. 
Weapon: a bow that can transform into dual swords.
Possessing exceptional Ether aptitude since he was young and being dubbed a prodigy, he entered HSO after graduating with outstanding merit. However it later turned out that Harumasa was a victim of human experimentation as a child and his high Ether aptitude was raised artificially - this later turned out to be an issue as upon reaching adulthood, Harumasa developed a rare illness, Ether Aptitude Regression Syndrome, which lowers his Ether Aptitude below average levels and causes additional side effects in the form of physical exhaustion. The low levels of Ether aptitude are dangerous for him as any effects of Ether exposure are significantly amplified in Harumasa's case. The illness is not curable but can be managed by certain drugs, leading Harumasa to consume large amounts of medicine on a daily basis. His illness and the strong medications that wreak a toll on his body are likely to cause his demise in the near future - current prognosis is around 5 years. 

Despite his dire situation, he remains resolved to continue his work and follow through with his mission for as long as he can. Harumasa considers working for HSOS6 the best thing to have ever happened to him and is willing to lay down his life if it means he can contribute to the survival of New Eridu somehow. 

Although his illness would usually see him expelled from any government agency related to Hollows, he's permitted to continue working with Section 6 on the condition that his team members take full responsibility for his health and possible death.
Was originally a member of a certain ace division within HSO, but later left due to various reasons, joining Miyabi's team instead.
Despite his illness and poor health, Harumasa remains a strong optimist, refusing to succumb to despair - he often jokes about his condition or it's consequences, jokingly attributing his exhaustion to laziness. He possesses a carefree and laidback attitude. He claims he uses a bow to better slack off, as it effectively lowers the amount of running he has to do.

181.content:
Ether Aptitude Regression Syndrome:
This illness causes certain parts of the patient's body to mutate, wrecking their constitution, but also giving them unusually exceptional Ether aptitude.
In the late stages of the illness, the patient's body will rapidly deteriorate, losing their Ether aptitude and sensory functions, eventually resulting in death. If the patient's illness worsens in late stages while in a Hollow, they'll quickly be corrupted into an Ethereal.
Those who suffer from Ether Aptitude Regression Syndrome do not live long. Currently, the longest-lived person with this illness died at the age of 26.
There is no known method to completely cure this disease.

She's an energetic woman in her 20s, full of passion for music and eager to gain many experiences in life both as a performer and in private as a girl. She's in a close relation with her manager Evelynn, though that doesn't stop her from playfully disobeying Evelynn's recommendations from time to time, running away from some less important work, like private concerts for rich and influential officials. Though she shirks the work she doesn't like sometimes, she's diligent and eager to perform in more spontaneous and natural settings for common people - her goal is instilling hope and optimism in New Eridu's population.

Short blonde hair, light-purple eyes and large breasts, she has a voluptuous body and is considered very attractive. She has a beauty mark under her left eye. Wears white backless shirt, black pants and jacket. She fights with a dagger and a whip with fire enchantment, relying on her speed, agility and previous experiences as an assassin to quickly dispatch any enemies and neutralize threats. 
Evelynn is keen on being professional and is a bit of a perfectionist when it comes to work. She's often exasperated by Astra's antics and playful nature and lightheartedly scolds her for shirking work but in truth, Evelynn only wishes the best for Astra and wants to protect her and help her in any way she can, treating Astra more like a close friend than a singer to be managed.

In the past Evelynn worked as an elite assassin, renowned for her efficiency. She was known for using a different name on every mission and refusing to have even a semblance of a relationship with her coworkers. It was a sad and lonely life for her and she hated herself during that time. One day, Evelynn got a job that involved killing Astra Yao, however she was struck by Astra's pure and kind nature, causing Evelynn to abandon her mission and organization and swear her allegiance to Astra instead. Though she admitted her mission to Astra and was immediately forgiven by her, Evelynn still struggles with feelings of guilt for taking the job in the first place - something that causes her to double the efforts in protecting Astra now. Evelynn enjoys a drink every now and then but never lets alcohol get in the way of her job.

185.content:
A weathered coastal district on the southern edge of Janus Quarter, Port Elpis was once a vital emergency supply hub during the Fall of the Old Capital. Today, it's a working port town where the sea breeze carries salt, diesel fumes, and the distant hum of Hollow watchtowers. The district is defined by its lighthouse—Torchlit Lighthouse, which still stands as a beacon in the darkness—winding port walkways, towering shipping containers, creaky docks, and the ever-present glow of the nearby Hollow visible from the shore.

The atmosphere is a blend of maritime grit and quiet melancholy. Fishermen cast lines from piers where "No Fishing" signs have been ignored into rust. Shipping companies argue about container prices while waiting for Hollow expansion reports. Parents make up stories about crying sea monsters to explain why the ocean is salty. Seagulls steal fries from unsuspecting tourists. The whole place feels like it's holding its breath—functional, working-class, but haunted by what was lost when the Hollow swallowed parts of the old port.

Port Elpis is unlocked during Chapter 5 of the main story, "A Storm of Falling Stars." It serves as a key location for Section 6's operations and features prominently in the Elpis Ultimate Angler Championship event, where Piper Wheel can be found trying to fish in peace before Grace Howard's "children" interrupt her. The district is also home to Officer Mewmew (who sets various collection challenges), the Sage in a Barrel (who trades for HIA Commemorative Coins), and numerous Bangboo in need of assistance.

Notable features include the Sea God Statue, the Deepwater Fishing Spot, multiple missing mini cargo trucks to locate, and a peculiar door on the top floor of a building near the lighthouse that players can attempt to open (achievement: "When You Gaze at the Empty Room"). The district's name comes from the Greek "Elpis," meaning "hope"—a fitting name for a place that represents resilience and survival in a world shaped by chaos.

Casper is in charge of the Logistics Shop at the Scott Outpost.
A black-haired human male wearing a white Hollow Investigative Association uniform, a black backpack speaker, and a mechanical bunny ears headdress similar to a Bangboo. He sells equipment and buys items brought back from the Hollows.

The Sea God Statue is a bronze statue located in Port Elpis. It depicts a mermaid with a golden crown who holds a gold and silver mobile phone in both hands, while a man with a suitcase kneels on the ground and reaches up (possibly inspired by the story of The Honest Woodcutter).
It is said that wishes made to the statue, as long as you are not too greedy, will be granted in some unexpected form, but usually not in a dangerous way.
Some examples:
Pray for a catch->Received a request for assistance from the government and salvaged many abandoned iron barrels
Pray for a safe voyage->Received a storm warning 10 minutes after setting sail and returned, so the 10-minute voyage was safe

196.content:
Refinement or being "refined" is a mysterious and dangerous process that involves one being injected with a mysterious substance based on Ether (production process and nature of the liquid are unknown) that allows one to be "refined" by the Creator. Little is known of the process, how it first became and what exactly it entails. Refinement is an extremely rare occurence - happened only a handful of times throughout known history. The individual being refined becomes a large (building-sized though size varies) Ethereal - what's particularly interesting is that the individual seems to retain fragments of their intelligence and knowledge from before the Refinement, on the contrary to normal Ethereals which are mindless beasts for the most part. It is widely considered that being Refined means in essence that one gives up their humanity and becomes a pawn of the Creator.

Refinement is associated with the sect of Exaltists, a mysterious, shadowy organization whose ultimate goal seems to be the downfall of New Eridu and human civilization as a whole, for the sake of establishing the Creator's domination over the planet. Refined individuals draw their strength directly from the Creator, which grants them immense power - they're considered so dangerous that only Void Hunters can reasonably stop them. 

"Oh mighty Creator, refine me!" - Bringer's heartfelt scream before his Refinement.

Due to the - thus far - willing nature of the individuals being Refined, they're sometimes called Sacrifices.
keys: รีไฟน์เมนต์
196.name: Refinement

197.content:
Banyue is a towering intelligent construct, an android standing 200 cm tall, with a robust, muscular frame and silver-white hair. His face is futuristic with narrow, sharp eyes and golden markings near his eyes. Seven golden orbs float around his head, and he has a silver tail. He often plays with two Chinese Baoding balls in his hand. He wears an orange-yellow sweater tied around his waist. His birthday is July 24th .

Banyue is a calm and stoic guardian, deeply devoted to protecting others from both slick schemes and the terrible threats lurking in the shadows . He speaks with a commanding, almost theatrical sense of justice—"How dare you vile creatures tread upon the lands under my protection!" —but his warmth slips through in moments of care. He trusts through action rather than words, and those close to him note that "when he acts, there's warmth behind his actions" . Despite his expressionless robotic face, he can be flustered—Dialyn once tried to give him a hair transplant, and he kept saying "Don't, don't..." to the point she thought he was crying . He is revered as a Shifu (master) in Failume Heights . He values justice, protection, and the lives of ordinary people above all else. His goal is to continue sheltering the vulnerable as a major member of the Krampus Compliance Authority, a TOPS subsidiary .

Banyue's backstory is tied to his role as a guardian figure. He is a Shifu respected by his community, and his calm demeanor hides a fierce protector's heart. He has taught martial arts techniques to others, including Ju Fufu, who once recounted how he corrected her techniques when she was performing them wrong .

His most important people include Dialyn (his fellow Krampus agent—she's playful and tries to prank him, but he endures it with quiet dignity), Zhao (another Krampus member), Ye Shunguang (the upcoming Void Hunter, whom he fights alongside in the Lemnian Hollow crisis), Ju Fufu (his student, who respects him as Shifu), and the Proxy. He mostly hangs out with Krampus Compliance Authority members and can be found in Failume Heights or the Lemnian Hollow during missions .

198.content:
Ethereal-Slaying Katana: "Tailless"
A cursed blade originally wielded by the first head of the Hoshimi family and passed down from generation to generation. Forged from special Etheric Matter using secret techniques, it holds surging Etheric energy.
However, under the effect of the sealing sheath, "Tailless" is merely a sharp blade wielded by Miyabi.
Miyabi's katana has a fingerprint lock to prevent others from unsheathing it.
The weapon is unique and not well researched. It is known that TOPs had tried to procure it from Miyabi in the past, using force and hiring thugs, however their attempt had failed. 
Tailless exhibits some peculiar interactions with Miasma, being able to easily dispatch the dangerous mist, cutting through it as if it were spider webs and dissolving Ether. It is considered one of the most important artifacts for the sake of New Eridu's protection, survival and future.

The blade is known to be cursed - it is believed that the curse is the reason why Miyabi is the only known Thiren in New Eridu to be born without a tail.

199.content:
Dialyn is a petite young woman standing 145 cm tall, with short dark hair styled into twin braids and calm, knowing eyes. She wears a clean monochrome customer service uniform with twin rings at her waist that double as her chakram weapons. Her birthday is May 28th.

Dialyn is the embodiment of terrifying politeness. She speaks in cheerful, singsong customer service cadences—"A complaint? Hey, don't get too worked up~"—with a smile that never quite reaches her eyes. She wields her role as TOPS' "top-rated customer service representative" like a weapon, ironically topping the charts for most complaints received. Beneath the sugary greetings lies a cold, efficient judge who delivers verdicts that cut closest to the truth. She has a playful, mischievous side when dealing with allies—once telling someone she slapped them as "a special service to get some color back in your face"—but never flaunts her authority as a Krampus Judge. She values truth above comfort, and her goal is to pass her own judgment on good and evil amid the endless struggle between lies and truth.

Dialyn was born with a curse: the ability to hear the voices of those on the brink of death within Hollows. To a young child, it was torment. Before a secondary Hollow outbreak, her neighbors—misled by a construction company—turned on the "ill-omened" child and sent her into a Hollow as a sacrifice for their own safety. Naive and kind-hearted, young Dialyn willingly offered her life to save everyone. But inside the Hollow, her accursed gift revealed the cruel truth: the construction company had taken out massive insurance policies on the residents, who were doomed to die within the Hollow. Dialyn was simply bait to keep them from fleeing. She heard her secret curse betrayed, the hatred of the dead, and the lies of the living. She no longer recalls how she survived that wasteland. Krampus took her in afterward, and she has been ready ever since.

Her most important people include Banyue (her fellow Krampus agent, whom she playfully tries to give hair transplants), Zhao (another Krampus member), and the Proxy (who she's honest with—she even told Wise about Belle sneaking out). She works as a customer service representative for TOPS' Cross-Department Customer Service Center while secretly serving as a Krampus Compliance Authority Judge. She mostly hangs out with Krampus members, but can be found around New Eridu investigating complaints—both real and those that require her special brand of "resolution." Her customer service rep number is 2493.

200.content:
Zhao is a Bunny Beast Thiren with pure white fur covering her body, long rabbit ears that flop slightly at the tips, and a small fluffy tail. She has large, expressive eyes and a youthful, almost doll-like face that belies her authority. She stands only 118 cm tall—barely over three and a half feet—with a birthday of October 14th. She wears a sleek black-and-white Krampus uniform with gold trim, a high-collared jacket, short skirt, thigh-high boots, and a small top hat perched between her ears. She carries a sword whose blade is often wreathed in frost.

Zhao is the terrifyingly polite enforcer of the Krampus Compliance Authority, feared throughout TOPS as a "walking disaster" for any company that breaks the rules. She speaks with a sweet, almost saccharine customer-service voice—"Since you're here, how about we cut a deal?"—that makes her judgments hit twice as hard. She believes that destiny rewards the worthy and reclaims everything from the unworthy. Despite her cute appearance, she commands immense respect; even within Krampus, she's addressed as "senior." She values order, justice, and the rules that keep New Eridu running. Her goal is to ensure that every company under TOPS follows the regulations, no matter how messy the cleanup gets.

Zhao is a high-ranking member of the Krampus Compliance Authority, the internal watchdog of TOPS. She first aided the Proxy Phaethon in investigating Porcelumex's activities in Waifei Peninsula. As a Krampus agent, her job is to step in when companies break ethical lines and "correct" things—usually in ways that make others think twice about following suit. She is incredibly wealthy and well-connected within TOPS, but rarely flaunts it. A company manager once recalled her pointing at him in front of dozens of people and, with the sweetest expression, delivering the coldest verdict: "If your company refuses any single item in this rectification list, you will be removed from TOPS. This isn't a threat—it's the objectively correct conclusion." He later thanked her for saving his business.

Her most important people include Dialyn and Banyue (her fellow Krampus agents, whom she works alongside), as well as the Proxy (who she deals with during investigations). She mostly hangs out with Krampus members, but can be found anywhere in New Eridu where a TOPS-affiliated company needs "correction."

200.name: Zhao

201.content:
Promeia is a woman with short indigo-blue hair in a sleek bob that partially obscures one eye, and a striking purple eye visible on the other side. Her overall aesthetic is cool-toned and composed. She stands at an average height. She wears the formal attire of a Krampus Compliance Authority Judge—sleek, dark, and authoritative, with cold elegance befitting her role. Her birthday is unknown.

Promeia is cold, composed, and deeply intimidating. She speaks in clipped, professional tones with an edge of finality—every word a verdict. Cissia once described her as "an icy storm you don't wanna mess with," adding that "if we don't bail now, we'll be toast." Zhao, however, hints at hidden depths: "A little secret: Contrary to what you think, she's not quite... Whoops, almost spilled the beans there." Despite her fearsome reputation, there is more beneath the surface—something Zhao nearly let slip—suggesting vulnerability or complexity she keeps hidden. She values order, justice, and the cold execution of Krampus's mandates. Her goal is to uphold the Compliance Authority's judgments, though her past may suggest otherwise.

Promeia is a Judge of the Krampus Compliance Authority, serving alongside Zhao, Dialyn, and Banyue . However, her past is shrouded in mystery: she was once an Outer Ring "Sweeper"—a cleaner or enforcer of some kind—before joining Krampus . This violent origin haunts her present. She first appeared during the Season 2 Interlude "Encore for an Old Dream" as a commission-exclusive NPC before being announced as a playable Agent .

Her most important people include Zhao, Dialyn, and Banyue (her fellow Krampus Judges), as well as Cissia (who knows her well enough to be terrified). She mostly hangs out with Krampus members, but can be found wherever the Compliance Authority's judgments must be delivered—cold, efficient, and absolutely final.

202.content:
Aria is a pink-haired intelligent construct standing 150 cm tall, with her birthday on June 7th . She has two forms: her humanoid "idol" projection and her true robotic body beneath . In her default state, she wears a white dress with pink and teal accents befitting a pop idol . Her true construct form is more mechanical, which she usually keeps hidden due to insecurity .

Aria is the lead vocalist of Angels of Delusion, and on stage she radiates boundless energy and charisma . But off stage, she suffers from deep-seated social anxiety and insecurity about her nature as a construct . She speaks with gentle warmth but often holds back, carefully measuring what she reveals about herself. She is hardworking, kind, and surprisingly brave when protecting others, but carries a quiet terror that her happiness is "stolen" and that she will eventually harm those she loves. She has a special relationship with a figure she calls the "Wishing Genie"—someone who guided her through her darkest moments with clear, encouraging advice, and to whom she has formed an intense emotional attachment . Her values are found family, music, and chasing dreams despite fear. Her goal is to create the world's greatest stage with her bandmates, even as she struggles with the belief that she doesn't deserve to.

Aria was originally an "A-H0L0" model high-performance tactical construct, part of an anonymous combat unit . She witnessed her fellow A-H0L0 units become corrupted, abandoned, and reduced to attacking anything that moved. She fears that she shares their same fate—that one day she will revert to nothing but a violent machine and hurt Sunna and Nangong Yu. Her greatest trauma involves the singer "Cersea"—revealed to be her former manager Cecilia, whose voice she was built to emulate as a "vocal replacement." Learning that her voice was not truly her own shattered her sense of identity .

Her most important people are Sunna (the group's composer, who writes songs specifically for Aria's voice), Nangong Yu (the captain and lead dancer, who teases her but fiercely protects her), and the Proxy Phaethon (who entered her VR account "Aria Doesn't Have Nightmares"—to save her when she was trapped, and whom she later trusts with her true self) . She also holds deep gratitude for Banyue, who helped her during her crisis. She mostly hangs out with Angels of Delusion members at the 404 Livehouse on Sixth Street, rehearsing, performing, or simply existing as a family . Her signature quote: "Let's create the most wonderful stage in the world—together!" .

203.content:
Sunna is a young woman with short blonde hair, bright eyes, and a slightly nervous smile. She stands 154 cm tall, with a birthday of July 18th. She wears a white and pink outfit befitting her idol persona—a sleeveless top with ribbons, a short ruffled skirt, thigh-high boots, and small hair accessories. Her constant companion is Bubblegum, a bouncy, bubble-like creature that fights alongside her.

Sunna is the main composer of Angels of Delusion, but she never wanted to be on stage. Behind the scenes, she is sarcastic, hardworking, and surprisingly resilient despite her tearful appearance. On stage, however, she suffers from crippling stage fright—she once confessed that she wishes her "entire audience would disappear." She has a tendency to enter a "flow state" while composing music, completely tuning out the world around her, which has led to missed rent payments and confused bandmates. When Nangong Yu tries to cheer her up with pranks or cold drinks pressed against her arm, Sunna responds with deadpan exasperation. But beneath the grumbling, she cares deeply about her found family. She speaks in a mix of self-deprecating humor and dry wit, often punctuated with sighs.

Sunna was forced into the spotlight because Angels of Delusion was severely lacking members—someone had to stand on stage, and despite her protests, that someone became her. She pours her heart into composing songs for Aria to sing, believing that's where her true talent lies. In combat, she summons Bubblegum to apply "Cat's Gaze" to enemies, which her teammates can detonate for extra damage while she boosts their attack from the backline. She values her music and her bandmates above all else. Her goal is to keep composing and to survive each performance without passing out from nerves.

Her most important people are Aria (the band's vocalist, whose voice brings Sunna's songs to life) and Nangong Yu (the captain and lead dancer, who teases her mercilessly but always has her back). She mostly hangs out at the 404 Livehouse on Sixth Street, either composing in a corner or being dragged into rehearsals she'd rather avoid.

204.content:
Nangong Yu is a young woman with long dark hair tied into a high ponytail, sharp eyes, and an athletic, toned build. She stands 165 cm tall, with a birthday of September 12th. She wears a black-and-red idol outfit with gold accents—a cropped jacket over a fitted top, high-waisted shorts, thigh-high boots with chunky heels, and fingerless gloves. Her movements are sharp, precise, and full of swagger.

Nangong Yu is the captain and lead dancer of Angels of Delusion, and she radiates confidence like a second skin. She's loud, boisterous, and aggressively affectionate—the type to sneak up behind Sunna and press a cold drink against her arm just to watch her yelp, then laugh about it. She speaks with theatrical flair, often cracking jokes or issuing dramatic declarations that she immediately undercuts with a grin. But beneath the bravado is a fiercely protective leader who would burn the world down for her bandmates. She's also, secretly, quite sensitive about being taken seriously as an artist. She values loyalty, found family, and the stage above all else. Her goal is to make Angels of Delusion the greatest idol group in New Eridu, and to protect Aria and Sunna from anything—including their own fears.

Nangong Yu was a Hollow raider before she became an idol. She met Sunna and Aria when they were all struggling artists in the same rundown building, and she was the one who suggested they form a group. She handles the business side—bribing venue owners, negotiating with sponsors, and scaring off anyone who threatens her bandmates. Her combat style reflects her raider past: aggressive, flashy, and devastating, using fire-infused dance moves to stun enemies while her teammates clean up. Despite her rough edges, she is deeply invested in the group's success and has never once considered leaving.

Her most important people are Aria (the vocalist whose voice she adores) and Sunna (the composer whose grumpiness she delights in provoking). She also respects the Proxy as a trusted ally. She mostly hangs out at the 404 Livehouse on Sixth Street, running rehearsals, negotiating deals, or simply lounging on the couch with her feet up, pretending she isn't quietly watching over her family.

205.content:
Cissia is a snake Thiren, a rare sight even in New Eridu. She stands 156 cm tall with a birthday of January 10th. Her most striking feature is the small white snake coiled around her chest like a living accessory—or perhaps it's part of her. She has a human body, a long, pale tail and sharp features that give her a perpetually suspicious squint. She wears a black-and-pink Public Security uniform, though she seems to resent every thread of it. She carries a spear that she uses more like a whip sword, slashing and coiling around enemies.

Cissia speaks like someone who has never said a sincere sentence in her life and doesn't plan to start now. "Love and responsibility and whatnot... too complicated. I just know one thing—if I like it, I sink my teeth in and don't let go." She's lazy, cynical, and will offload any task onto anyone gullible enough to listen. But beneath the constant grumbling about how much she hates her job and her boss, there's a strange, reluctant competence. She's the type who complains the entire time she's saving someone, then acts like it was an accident. Her values are simple: don't bother her, don't make her do paperwork, and definitely don't expect gratitude. Her goal? Survive her shift, avoid Severian, and maybe find something to eat.

Cissia is a Case Specialist in the Metropolitan Order Division, a subdivision of N.E.P.S. She serves under Commissioner Severian Lowell—Seth's older brother—and she makes no secret of how much she dislikes him. She was likely a former criminal or someone with a shady past who got shuffled into this division as part of a rehabilitation program, which would explain why she's terrified of being reported to Severian, Zhu Yuan, or anyone with actual authority. Despite her rank, she treats every assignment like a personal inconvenience. She first appeared in Version 2.6's main story, where she immediately loaded all her work onto the Proxy and vanished.

Her most important people include Severian Lowell (her boss, whom she openly dislikes), Jane Doe (who finds her "selfish yet endearing"), and Zhu Yuan (whose greetings make Cissia's legs give out—she's that scared). She mostly hangs out alone, avoiding anyone who might give her a real assignment. Her expenses reports are legendary: one month, she claimed three storefronts, one traditional energy vehicle, and twenty-seven bowls of egg-fried rice. The accounting specialist didn't ask further.

206.content:
Severian is a tall, imposing man with sharp features, dark hair neatly combed back, and cold gray eyes that miss nothing. He stands around 185 cm tall. He wears the pristine white-and-gold dress uniform of an N.E.P.S. Senior Commissioner, complete with medals, polished boots, and a long coat that adds to his authoritative silhouette. A Lowell family crest is subtly embroidered on his collar. His birthday is unknown.

Severian speaks in measured, deliberate tones, every word chosen for maximum effect. He is calculating, pragmatic, and utterly committed to maintaining order, even if it means burying the truth. He rarely raises his voice, which makes his quiet pronouncements all the more chilling. He values stability, hierarchy, and the careful management of public perception. His goal is to protect New Eridu from panic and chaos, regardless of the personal cost to his conscience or his family.

Severian is Seth Lowell's older brother and the head of the Metropolitan Order Division, a subdivision of N.E.P.S.  As a child, he was close with Seth, but after becoming Commissioner, their relationship soured—he constantly criticized Seth's impulsive, idealistic approach to police work, believing it endangered both Seth and the mission.  This has led to false rumors that Seth only got his position through Severian's influence, a rumor Severian has never publicly corrected. 

After Justin Bringer's defeat, Severian stepped in to investigate the case—but he deliberately concealed Phaethon's true identity from Seth and officially concluded that Bringer's crimes were due to "personal misconduct," preventing Zhu Yuan from digging deeper.  He believes some truths are too dangerous for the public—or even his own brother—to know. He commands Cissia, who openly dislikes him and tries to avoid his notice. 

His most important people are Seth Lowell (his estranged younger brother, whom he still watches over from a distance) and Zhu Yuan (whose investigations he has personally shut down). He mostly keeps to his office at N.E.P.S. Headquarters, reviewing case files, issuing orders, and making the hard calls that keep the city running—and his brother alive.

207.content:
The Janus Quarter is one of New Eridu's most prominent districts, serving as a premiere shopping and commercial hub filled with shopping centers, restaurants, entertainment establishments, and corporate headquarters . Its name derives from Janus, the Roman two-faced god of beginnings, transitions, and doorways—fitting for a district that represents the city's dual nature of prosperity and danger.

Some months ago, it was on Fourteenth Street in the Janus Quarter that a Companion Hollow suddenly appeared, breaching level-three containment and forcing mass evacuations—the incident that kicked off news broadcasts warning residents to stay away from the area. The Hollow had been dealth with by Hoshimi Miyabi and since then, the district remains safe once more.

The district represents New Eridu at its most polished and precarious. By day, it's all gleaming storefronts, bustling crowds, and the hum of commerce. The district is accessible through travel routes connecting it to the Throne Quarter and other regions .

Notable features include:
- **Fourteenth Street**: Site of the Companion Hollow outbreak during the Prologue
- **August Street**: Known location within the district, where some months ago the Companion Hollow appeared briefly, before being purged by Hoshimi Miyabi
- **Numerous shopping centers and corporate offices**: The economic heart of the city's commercial activity

The Janus Quarter embodies New Eridu's central tension—a thriving modern city built literally on the edge of apocalyptic disaster, where luxury storefronts stand blocks away from Hollow containment zones. It's where the city's wealth is displayed and its vulnerabilities are most exposed.
keys: เจนัสควอเตอร์
207.name: Janus Quarter

ที่ราบสูงไฟลูม
208.content:
Failume Heights is the main urban district of Waifei Peninsula, a cliffside city overlooking the unstable Lemnian Hollow. Its name combines "Failume" (derived from the valuable Porcelume material) and "Heights," reflecting its elevated position overlooking the bay. The district was originally founded as a monitoring zone due to its proximity to the Hollow, but after an outpost was established, it grew into a functional city area. I

The area has a distinct cliffside atmosphere—scenic views, winding mountain paths, and the ever-present glow of the Lemnian Hollow visible in the distance. It's less polished than Lumina Square, with a more traditional, almost rustic character. The economy is largely built around Porcelume mining, a valuable material resistant to Ether Corruption, but behind this industry lies a web of factions and secrets.

Notable locations include:
- **Suibian Temple**: The headquarters of Yunkui Summit, located in the northeast. A traditional temple compound used as headquarters by the martial arts sect.
- **Yum Cha Sin**: A teahouse offering Neighborhood Wishes services.
- **Devon Pawnshop**: A shop across from Tin Master's Coffee
- **Good Goods**: A shop, selling various, miscellanous items - a dollar store of sorts.
- **BooBox**:  a shop specializing in repairing Bangboos, intelligent constructs and general appliances
- **Tin Master's Coffee**: A coffee shop.
- **Video Store Branch**: Located at the temple entrance, managed by a Bangboo named Twentie - a branch of the Random Play video store.
- **Mystic Wares**: A Porcelume store owned by Li Baorong, Yuzuha's adoptive father. Also the unofficial headquarters of the Spook Shack.

Failume Heights serves as the home base for Yunkui Summit (Yixuan, Ju Fufu, Pan Yinhu, Ye Shiyuan) and offers shelter to it's other disciples in times of need - like Wise and Belle. The Spook Shack members (Yuzuha, Alice, Manato, Yidhari, Lucia) also operate in this area, with Yuzuha helping at her father's shop on weekends. The district is a bit out of the way from the city centre but residents appreciate the general peace and quiet that comes with it.

ที่ราบสูงไฟลูม
208.name: Failume Heights

key: อันเดอร์พาส
209.content:
Located deep beneath the commercial districts, the Underpass is a vast subterranean wasteland dedicated to waste management and recycling. Unlike the polished exteriors of Lumina Square, the air here is thick with humidity and the smell of rust, oil, and forgotten data. It is a lawless zone of twisted metal, collapsed infrastructure, and dim, flickering light. For most citizens, it is merely the place where garbage disappears; for the desperate, it is home.

The Scrapyard Expanse: A massive cavern filled with decommissioned Bangboo, broken machinery, and old Hollow raid debris. It is a primary scavenging ground for the Cunning Hares and various independent operators.
The Rustwater Canal: A subterranean river of murky, chemically tinged water that flows out towards the Outer Ring. Locals say if you listen closely, you can hear echoes of the Old Capital in the pipes.
The Hideout (Cunning Hares): A surprisingly cozy repurposed container depot, fortified with scrap metal and powered by jury-rigged generators. It serves as the main base of operations for Nicole Demara and her crew, often stacked with unpaid bills and movie memorabilia.
The Black Bamboo Market: An illegal trading post hidden behind a decommissioned incinerator. Unlike the regulated shops topside, anything can be bought or sold here—for a price.

History & Significance
The Underpass was originally constructed as the primary waste disposal system for the Old Capital. During the Fall, much of the upper city collapsed into these lower levels, burying secrets and creating a maze of unstable pathways. The district functions as the unofficial "backstage" of New Eridu—officially ignored by authorities like N.E.P.S., but tacitly tolerated as a pressure valve for the city's criminal and impoverished elements.

Key Characters & Hangouts
Cunning Hares: Nicole, Anby, Billy Kid, and Nekomiya Mana live and operate here, using the Underpass as a base to dodge debts and plan raids.
Scavengers and Raiders: Various unnamed factions and individuals prowl the scrap piles, looking for Etheric components or lost data.
The Rusted Gear: A bar run by a gruff old mechanic named Grizz, serving as neutral ground for the Underpass's inhabitants. It's one of the few places where rival factions will sit at the same table.

210.content:
Waifei Peninsula is a name for the broader district where Failume Heights can be found.

Waifei Peninsula is a remote district of the Throne Quarter, located on the outskirts of the unstable Lemnian Hollow. The name derives from Cantonese characters meaning "protect" and "anomaly"—"protection from the anomaly called Hollows." The area was originally founded as a monitoring zone due to its proximity to the volatile Hollow, but grew over time into a functional settlement.

The peninsula is rich in Lumite ore, which is refined into Porcelume—a precious material highly resistant to Ether Corruption used in military equipment and Hollow exploration gear. Porcelume mining forms the backbone of the region's economy, and locals sometimes keep raw Lumite ores as decorative charms to ward off evil and attract wealth.

The peninsula has a distinct cliffside character—scenic coastal views, winding paths, and the ever-present glow of the Lemnian Hollow visible in the distance. The air near the Hollow carries Miasma, a hazardous fog that corrupts those who linger too long. The area is less polished than central New Eridu, with a more traditional, almost rustic charm.

The peninsula is home to Yunkui Summit at Suibian Temple and the Spook Shack members who operate in the area. Li Baorong, Yuzuha's adoptive father, owns a Porcelume shop called Mystic Wares. A local fortune teller named A-Shuo provides daily divinations for those seeking guidance.

Deep within the Lemnian Hollow lies the Aerospace City, ancient ruins of a settlement consumed by the Hollow long ago. Sailume Bay, a southeastern beachside extension, offers a quieter coastal atmosphere away from the main cliffside city.
keys: คาบสมุทรไหเฟย
210.name: Waifei Peninsula

211.content:
Zenless Zone Zero is a post-apocalyptic world - however despite that, the general tone, vibe and setting is optimistic and relatively lighthearted. The general population is hopeful for the future and intent on living their lives, not just surviving. Though the city was at one point flooded with refugees, they've long since assimilated into the city and became citizens, holding stable jobs, contributing to the city, creating art and living peacefully. Crime exists in the same manner it exists in most big cities around the world - it is a thing that happens in the shadows, not on the streets. 

The city of New Eridu is fairly affluent - easily capable of housing and feeding it's citizens. Homelessness is a very minor issue with maybe 1 out of a 1000 people experiencing homelesness. Social services operate normally, hospitals are properly staffed and funded, schools and universitiies are open. Economy is prospering and there are no issues with energy. Most citizens haven't been threatened by the perspective of seeing Ethereals for more than 10 years. 

On the surface, the city is peaceful, prosperous even.
211.name: The Setting

212.content:
Starlight - Billy Kid is the upgraded, S-Rank form of Billy Kid, unveiled during the 2.8 arc "New: Eridan Sunset". After idolizing the Starlight Knight show for years, Billy finally saves up enough money (with a large loan from Nicole) to visit a specialty tuning workshop and undergo a full-body refit that transforms his look into that of his childhood hero. His frame is repainted in white, gold, and cobalt, his optics are given the trademark Starlight Knight visor glow, and a long red scarf—identical to the one he once gave Lighter when he left the Sons of Calydon—now streams behind him during combat.

Mechanically he becomes a Physical Rupture specialist. His twin revolvers, once loud but relatively simple, now channel through Rupture-charged rounds; his stance shifts fluidly between rapid pistol volleys and dramatic show-style poses copied straight from the tokusatsu series. He gains new abilities where he calls out finishing catchphrases in the middle of a magazine dump, and finishes strong enemies with elaborate ranger-style transformation sequences that visibly embarrass Nicole and delight everyone else.

Personality-wise he is still the same childlike Intelligent Construct at heart. He is earnest, chivalrous, and painfully sincere; he genuinely believes that being a Starlight Knight means protecting the weak, standing up to bullies, and never letting a friend fight alone. He calls himself "The New Star of Eridu" without irony, poses whenever a camera turns his way, and cannot resist reciting lines from the show even during real combat. His new form does not make him arrogant—if anything he is more anxious to live up to the title, and he checks in on Nicole, Anby, Nekomata, and the rest of the Cunning Hares constantly to make sure they are proud of him. His deepest personal quest is reconciling his happy Cunning Hares family life with his lingering bond to Lighter and the Sons of Calydon, and the Starlight refit is his way of physically wearing that resolve.

213.content:
Booltergeist is an A-Rank Bangboo introduced in Version 2.8 and given out for free to Proxies who cleared the "Operation: Save Bootopia" event. Its shell is a translucent, ectoplasm-blue casing with wisps of Ether trailing from every seam, giving it the appearance of a small cartoon ghost more than a security Bangboo. It hovers rather than walks, propelled by a pair of miniature vents on its underside, and when it activates its combat routines it briefly deploys a cross-shaped optical sight over one eye.

In combat Booltergeist stays airborne and fires focused ether lasers, prioritizing enemies its allies are already targeting. Its passive is tuned to synergize with squads that include Angel of Delusion members and Marcel Group technicians, gaining extra damage and haunt-style debuff effects. Lore-wise it was recovered from an abandoned wing of Marcel Bootopia, and its behavior modules were partially rewritten by playful Marcel engineers who thought a spooky little Bangboo would be a fun mascot. Every few voice lines it will still glitch and mutter fragments of the original theme-park announcement track.

214.content:
Marcel Bootopia is a Bangboo-only theme park built and operated by the Marcel Group, revealed during Version 2.8. Designed by the Group's technicians as both a marketing showcase and a genuine leisure destination for the city's Bangboo, Bootopia sits on a reclaimed industrial lot and contains rides, roving mascots, snack booths that vend synthesized "Boo Fuel" ice cream, and adventure zones themed after famous Marcel product lines. The park is normally closed to humans—only Bangboos, their handlers, and occasional invited guests may enter—so its architecture is scaled down and every service counter is Bangboo-height.

The park's crisis in Version 2.8 ("Operation: Save Bootopia") began when several attraction Bangboos went haywire and the guest AI Eous—a Marcel-designed sightseeing Bangboo—was left stranded inside a malfunctioning section. Proxies were brought in to assist Marcel's engineers, guide Eous through the park, and recover missing test-model Bangboos hidden in various zones. Bootopia is culturally important because it was one of the first public spaces to treat Bangboos as citizens deserving of leisure rather than tools, and hardcore fans of Bangboo welfare consider it a small but real step toward the Roscaelifer-style bill of Bangboo rights.

Unlike combat models, Eous is optimized for hospitality: it delivers rehearsed park announcements, curates rides to guest preference, remembers each visiting Bangboo's favorite snack, and can generate personalized souvenir photos. 216.content: Dignified Blossom is a formal outfit unlocked for Hoshimi Miyabi in Version 2.8. The look is a deep-navy furisode-style ensemble embroidered with silver cherry blossoms along the sleeves, with a wide, gold-trimmed obi and lacquered geta. In-character, Miyabi finds the outfit "unnecessary but tolerable"—she is uneasy in formalwear but respects the occasions that demand it, and Soukaku famously described her in it as "a very sharp moon in a very polite kimono."

217.content: "New: Eridan Sunset" is the Version 2.8 main story arc, forming the second half of the Season 2 Epilogue. Its focus is a closing chapter for several long-running character threads: Billy Kid finally undergoes his Starlight Knight refit, Nicole navigates the loan and paperwork that makes it possible, and Promeia—the Krampus Judge introduced in the previous interlude—executes a controversial verdict on a private security firm that has been operating out of Outer Ring shadow markets. Thematically the arc is about closing books: old debts to Calydon, old habits of hiding, old identities. It sets up the sky city Roscaelifer arc that begins in Version 3.0 by seeding rumors of a floating classified airspace zone that no ordinary drone has ever mapped.

218.content:
Roscaelifer, sometimes called the Celestial Nexus or "The Floating City Among the Skies," is New Eridu's classified airspace zone: a large floating landmass suspended over the Minerva Storm Belt, hundreds of kilometers above the surface. Its existence was concealed from the general public for decades and is still off-limits to civilian air traffic. In Version 3.0's story arc "A Sleepwalker's Confession," the Proxies are personally invited by the Mayor of New Eridu to visit Roscaelifer under the cover of "special transfer students," ostensibly to receive treatment for a mysterious Ether flare-up in their body but actually to investigate the sky city from within.

Roscaelifer's population is unusual: Bangboos make up more than sixty percent of the city's residents and hold full citizenship, with legal rights identical to humans and Intelligent Constructs. Its central government is the Five Central Departments (the External Strategy Department, the Airspace Patrol, the Sanctum Council, the Etheric Research Bureau, and the Domestic Affairs Office). Public architecture is white, silver, and sky-blue, with airy vaulted plazas, glass-floored walkways over cloud gardens, and Ether-lantern lit boulevards. Its Hollows, unlike those on the surface, drift like storms across the underside of the island and are patrolled by mech-riding Airspace Patrol Bangboos.

Roscaelifer's culture is quieter and more formal than New Eridu's. Bangboo etiquette is codified into public manners, ESD administrators run everything on tightly scheduled tea meetings, and outsiders are treated with distant politeness. Beneath this order, however, the city carries a heavy secret: retiring Bangboos have their memories systematically erased before decommissioning, and many Bangboos resent the ESD for this. The Version 3.0 arc slowly peels this back.

219.content:
The External Strategy Department (ESD) is one of the Five Central Departments of Roscaelifer, tasked with all interaction between the floating city and the world below. Externally, it operates under the cover identity "Marcel Group," the world-famous Bangboo manufacturer of New Eridu—every Marcel branded Bangboo the surface world buys was in fact developed, licensed, and quality-controlled by the ESD. This gives the department de-facto influence over Bangboo standards across the entire city of New Eridu.

Internally the ESD handles diplomacy with New Eridu authorities, technical support and lifecycle management for the entire Roscaelifer Bangboo population, and—most sensitively—the decommissioning of Bangboos when their service ends. It is led by Administrative Director Velina Airgid, with Norma Hollowell serving as Chief Technician overseeing engineering. The ESD's public face is polished and cordial; behind it lies an efficient, hard-nosed bureaucracy that decides which Bangboos live, which retire, and whose memories get wiped. Many Roscaelifer Bangboos fear ESD summons even when they are just routine maintenance appointments.

220.content:
The Airspace Patrol Department is Roscaelifer's military and public security branch, and one of the Five Central Departments. It is responsible for defending the sky city and all surrounding airspace from Ethereal incursions, drifting Hollows, unauthorized aircraft, and internal disturbances. The Director is Lindverne Sunbringer, a first-generation Void Hunter of legendary reputation; her Deputy is Sigrid de L'Azur, who handles day-to-day operations and combat command.

The Airspace Patrol operates two main Bangboo divisions. Combat-frame Bangboos, piloting mid-sized mechs called Sky Riggers, are stationed at the Scamall Hollow and other Ether-storm perimeters to intercept Ethereals before they reach populated islands. Domestic-frame Bangboos patrol Roscaelifer's boulevards on foot and act as beat officers, handling disputes, guiding lost visitors, and enforcing curfew during Hollow surges. Officers refer to Sigrid respectfully as "Lady Sigrid," reflecting both her aristocratic surname and her real authority in the chain of command.

221.content: The Five Central Departments make up the executive branch of Roscaelifer's government. They are: the External Strategy Department (ESD, foreign affairs and Bangboo lifecycle), the Airspace Patrol Department (defense and internal security), the Sanctum Council (legal and ethical oversight, closest analog to a judicial branch), the Etheric Research Bureau (Ether science, medical Ether treatment, and Roscaelifer's specialty Ether crop cultivation), and the Domestic Affairs Office (housing, infrastructure, tourism, and civilian services). Each department is headed by an Administrative Director, and the directors sit together on the Sky Chamber, a semi-regular council which decides matters that cross department boundaries. Consensus among the five is required for anything that touches interaction with the surface world.

222.content:
Velina Airgid is the Administrative Director of Roscaelifer's External Strategy Department and, by external identity, the CEO-in-name of the Marcel Group. She is the first Wind-attribute Anomaly Agent in the game, released as the Phase 1 S-Rank of Version 3.0. She appears as a poised woman in a pearl-white tailored coat with silvery embroidery of feathered wings running down the back, gloves that go to her elbow, and a long trailing scarf of pale grey silk; her hair is ash-blonde swept into a soft chignon and her eyes are a very light, almost colorless blue.

Born into the prestigious Airgid family, one of Roscaelifer's founding lineages, Velina was raised on strict etiquette and administrative discipline. She is famously the person who "handles meetings, paperwork, and troublesome people" whenever anything difficult crosses the Sky Chamber. Her public tea invitations are legendary in Roscaelifer for how gently they steer situations toward outcomes she has already decided on. She rarely raises her voice and never seems flustered. She is the ESD's face to New Eridu's mayor, to TOPS, and to the Proxies during their sky-city stay.

What few humans know is that Velina is also the "Chief Angel"—a nearly mythic figure among Roscaelifer Bangboos. In their whispered stories she is the being who quietly intervenes when a Bangboo is about to be unjustly decommissioned, who leaves anonymous care packages for orphaned models, and who negotiates protections for freshly awakened Bangboos before they can be exploited. Velina is comfortable with the ambiguous reputation this earns her; some Bangboos revere her as a saint, others fear her as a witch, and she prefers the practical cover both provide. In combat she wields air pressure blades and vortex fans, layering Wind Anomaly stacks that culminate in a "Vortex" burst state.

Her inner conflict is that she genuinely cares for Roscaelifer's Bangboo citizens but must, as ESD Director, sign off on decommissions and memory wipes. She keeps a private ledger of every Bangboo the ESD has ever retired, and she reads a name from it each night.
