223.content:
Norma Hollowell is the Chief Technician of the External Strategy Department and a central engineering figure of the Marcel Group. She is an S-Rank Fire Stun Agent, released as the Phase 2 banner of Version 3.0. She appears as a young woman in her mid-twenties with unruly copper-red hair kept out of her face by a pair of aviator goggles pushed up on her forehead, a fire-resistant technician's jumpsuit worn open to the waist over a black tank top, heavy work gloves, and grease smudges she never quite manages to wipe away. She stands around 168 cm.

Norma is the classic "genius scientist" archetype dialed up: her engineering skill is arguably the sharpest in Roscaelifer, and her people skills are inversely proportional. As a young lead researcher she pushed the performance ceilings of mass-produced Bangboo far beyond what her predecessors thought possible, and every commercial Marcel model on New Eridu's streets today runs firmware she personally refined. She talks quickly, gestures with tools she's still holding, and forgets meetings, mealtimes, and once—famously—her own birthday party.

Where Velina is subtle, Norma is the opposite: emotional, blunt, and terrible at hiding her feelings about the ESD's decommissioning policies. She has openly wept at retirement ceremonies and has been reprimanded (gently, by Velina) for smuggling failing Bangboos into her private workshop to keep them running past their scheduled deactivation date. She loves the city's Bangboos as a family and is unusually sensitive to their resentment of the ESD; she is Roscaelifer's most vocal internal reformer.

In combat she carries a shoulder-mounted heavy launcher and a portable ordnance rig, and after any teammate stuns an enemy she calls in an autonomous missile bombardment—six seconds of continuous fire-attribute strikes on random enemies in the zone. Her signature move is called "Full Ignition Kickoff" and involves lighting the fuse of a rocket she is riding.

224.content: The Airgid family is one of the founding lineages of Roscaelifer, dating back to the sky city's original engineering charter. Historically they were airfoil physicists, atmospheric ether-flow theorists, and administrators—unglamorous but indispensable to keeping a floating city from falling out of the sky. Over time they consolidated administrative power rather than technical fame, and today the Airgids sit at the top of Roscaelifer's polite society. The family is famous for a rigidly ceremonial upbringing: heirs are drilled in etiquette, negotiation, five-language protocol, and how to run meetings that never appear to be tense even when they are. Velina Airgid is the current head-of-generation, and her surname alone opens (or closes) most doors on the island.

225.content:
In Roscaelifer, Bangboos are not tools—they are citizens. More than sixty percent of the sky city's population are Bangboos, and by law they hold the same rights as humans and Intelligent Constructs: they may own property, vote in ward elections, marry (in the Bangboo-recognized sense), work, and file grievances. The city's ambient culture reflects this. Public transit has Bangboo-height handles, restaurants list Ether-fuel menus alongside human cuisine, and civic holidays include the annual Awakening Day when the first mass production of self-aware Bangboo units is commemorated.

The darker side of Bangboo society is the retirement protocol. To keep Roscaelifer's technology secret, every Bangboo whose service ends—whether by mechanical wear, obsolescence, or personal choice—must have their memory core wiped before leaving the city. Most Roscaelifer Bangboos accept this the way surface humans accept mortality; a small but vocal underground refuses. Rumors of a "Chief Angel" who quietly helps Bangboos evade the wipe have circulated for years, feeding both hope and superstition.

226.content:
Pyrois is the free Season 3 launch S-Rank Agent given to every Proxy, obtained through the Version 3.0 main story chapter "A Sleepwalker's Confession." Pyrois is not a human, a Thiren, or a construct—she is an Etheric Other, a manifestation of the dormant Legacy of Helios that had lain quiescent inside the Proxy Phaethon since the Season 2 climax. Under a critical Ether flare-up on Roscaelifer, that legacy finally externalizes as a distinct combat-avatar whose loyalty is to Belle and Wise directly.

Visually she is a slender figure clad in matte-black plating stylized like harness and racing tack, with a molten-orange crest running along her spine and pauldrons that flare into stylized flames. Her weapon is a whip that ignites into a solar filament in mid-arc. Her mythological namesake, Pyrois, is one of the four immortal horses that pull the chariot of the Sun in classical myth—"the fiery one"—which the Marcel Group's designers noted with dry amusement when comparing her to Roscaelifer's other sun-named figures.

Personality-wise Pyrois is protective, blunt, and impatient. She speaks with the tempo of someone who has been silent for a very long time and is now finally allowed to. She refers to Belle and Wise as "my Phaethon" and treats their safety as her only real priority; she is polite to their allies but not warm. Her mood improves considerably in the presence of Bangboos, whom she seems to instinctively like. She is also the first character to visibly react to Sunbringer, the first-generation Void Hunter, as if she recognizes her—suggesting the Legacy of Helios's memory extends much further back than anyone had assumed.

227.content:
The Legacy of Helios is the dormant power carried inside the Proxy Phaethon (Belle and Wise), whose true nature became clear only in Version 3.0. It is an ancient etheric inheritance from a source referred to in the game's mythology as Helios, the Sun—an entity whose relationship to the old civilization, the Collapse, and the first generation of Void Hunters is still being unraveled. In practical terms the Legacy is the reason the Proxies are able to survive Ether densities that would incapacitate anyone else, the reason they can synchronize with Bangboos so unusually well, and the reason their bodies periodically overload with heat and light during severe stress.

Before Version 3.0 the Legacy expressed only as background aptitude. In "A Sleepwalker's Confession" it externalizes as the Etheric Other Pyrois. Roscaelifer's Etheric Research Bureau considers the Legacy the most important unknown quantity in modern Ether science. Remielle Dan and Lindverne Sunbringer, two of the first-generation Void Hunters, appear to recognize the Legacy on sight—implying it has been walking among humans for far longer than the Proxies' own lifetimes.

228.content:
"A Sleepwalker's Confession" is the Version 3.0 main story arc and the first chapter of Season 3. The Proxies suffer a severe Ether flare-up whose source cannot be identified by any of the Section 6, HIA, or TOPS specialists in New Eridu. Mayor's Office pulls strings and invites the Proxies to Roscaelifer, the classified sky city, on the pretext of a very selective transfer-student program run by the Etheric Research Bureau. There they are placed under the wing of the External Strategy Department's Velina Airgid, introduced to the Airspace Patrol under Lindverne Sunbringer, and slowly drawn into three converging crises: a wave of Bangboo self-detonations, anomalous surges in the Energy Hub Hollows below the island, and the recurring appearance of the long-vanished first-generation Void Hunter Remielle Dan.

The arc culminates in the Legacy of Helios inside the Proxies awakening as the Etheric Other Pyrois, and in the reveal that Roscaelifer's peace is bought at the cost of routine Bangboo memory-wipes. It sets up the season's throughline of "whose sky is this, really"—the question of who Roscaelifer belongs to, its human founding families, its Bangboo majority, or the older forces that preceded both.

229.content: The Energy Hub Hollows are the drifting, storm-like Hollows that hang beneath and around Roscaelifer, feeding on the island's Ether hub and periodically discharging Ethereals into its lower districts. Unlike surface Hollows, they are three-dimensional and mobile, drifting through the cloud strata like slow, luminous jellyfish; they cannot be contained with fixed barriers, only intercepted by patrols. The Airspace Patrol maintains a permanent picket of Bangboo-piloted Sky Riggers around them. In Version 3.0 the Energy Hub Hollows begin behaving abnormally—expanding, discharging Ethereals in unusual patterns, and pulling Ether density from the city core—which is one of the crises that draws the Proxies to Roscaelifer.

230.content: The Scamall Hollow is the largest of Roscaelifer's drifting Hollows, a slow-rotating Ether storm suspended within the Minerva Storm Belt. Its name comes from the Airgid family's old tongue and translates approximately as "the cloud that dreams." It is the primary station of the Airspace Patrol's combat Bangboo garrison, who pilot Sky Riggers to intercept the Ethereals it disgorges. Scamall is stable enough to be lived alongside but too dense to be entered casually; Airspace Patrol officers rotate short shifts and are pulled out at the first sign of Ether saturation.

231.content: The Minerva Storm Belt is the ring of perpetual high-altitude Ether storms that circles the region above New Eridu at Roscaelifer's altitude. It has kept the sky city hidden from ground observation for decades: no aircraft can safely traverse the Belt without a Roscaelifer-issued flight envelope, and radar returns from within it are effectively random. Ether saturation in the Belt is what allows Roscaelifer to "float" in the first place—its buoyancy is not mechanical but etheric. The Belt is patrolled by Airspace Patrol drones and its edges are shifting Hollow spawn grounds.

232.content:
Remielle Dan is a first-generation Void Hunter and the Phase 1 S-Rank Agent of Version 3.1, the game's second anniversary patch. She is over a century old but appears to be in her mid-twenties, physically preserved by mechanisms that neither New Eridu nor Roscaelifer's Etheric Research Bureau can fully explain. She is tall, with long straight ink-black hair, a red silk half-mask that covers the lower half of her face, and dark tactical robes worn over close-fitting armor plating. Her signature weapon is a curved single-edged sword whose sheath doubles as a rifle stock.

Her legend predates modern New Eridu. Alongside Vike, the founding leader of the Falkenhayn Mercenary Troupe, and other first-generation hunters, Remielle helped push back the Dark Wall by roughly thirty-seven kilometers during the first years after the Collapse—one of the largest single reclamations in Void Hunter history. After the campaign she vanished; she was assumed dead by most historians and treated as legend by hobbyists.

In Version 2.x cameos and the Version 3.0 arc she reappears without explanation in Roscaelifer, watching from balconies and rooftops, refusing all interviews, and displaying an almost obsessive interest in Lindverne Sunbringer. It is revealed that a century ago Remielle came into direct conflict with Sunbringer—another first-generation Void Hunter—for reasons Remielle refuses to fully discuss, and that Remielle attempted to kill her and failed. That unresolved fight is the reason Remielle has returned.

Her personality is calm, blunt, and older than she looks. She has patience for the young, respect for the competent, and no time for polite lies. She is fond of tea, silence, and old books, and is quietly loyal to those few she still considers friends. Her attribute in combat is a new star-shaped element—a variant on Lumiflux—that reacts with the Anomaly system to enable hyper-carry damage tied to her elaborate combo strings.

233.content:
Sigrid de L'Azur is the Phase 2 S-Rank Agent of Version 3.1 and the Deputy Administrative Director of the Airspace Patrol Department. She is a Horse Thiren of considerable elegance, standing 172 cm with a long chestnut braid, tall pointed horse-ears that flick when she is amused, a tufted tail, and pale blue eyes. She wears a dark blue-and-silver uniform in a stylized cavalry cut with a knee-length coat, cavalry boots, and a shoulder-mounted authority sash. Her weapon is a two-and-a-half meter cavalry lance whose head can rotate into a piercing drill.

Sigrid is by-the-book to the point of exasperation. Rules are, to her, a moral instrument—she believes that a well-designed rule set is how a modern civilization avoids the mistakes of the Collapse—and so she follows procedure meticulously and expects her subordinates to do the same. Airspace Patrol officers call her "Lady Sigrid" both because her surname is aristocratic and because she has genuinely earned the honorific through her professional command. Off-duty she is much softer: she is an accomplished baker (her strawberry-and-cardamom scones are famous inside the Patrol), an enthusiastic knitter who has personally made sweaters for half her squad's Bangboos, and a very quiet reader of romance serials.

Her combat kit is built around alternating Knight Skills to charge Alt Attack stacks. Each Knight Skill she uses adds a stack of "Charge," and once fully stacked her Alt Attack becomes a devastating ice-attribute lance strike. She is protective of her subordinate Bangboos and openly refuses missions she considers wastefully dangerous—a habit that puts her at professional odds with Lindverne Sunbringer but that has earned her deep loyalty from her troops.

234.content:
Lindverne Sunbringer is the Director of Roscaelifer's Airspace Patrol Department and the second known first-generation Void Hunter to reappear in modern times, alongside Remielle Dan. She goes by the title "Sunbringer"—a name shared with the legendary figure appearing in older lorebook entries; the shared name is not coincidental and hints at a bloodline or covenant that predates the Collapse. She is a stern, imposing woman in her apparent thirties, tall, with long platinum-blonde hair worn in a warrior's braid down her back, gold-rimmed pauldrons over a formal Airspace Patrol coat, and a two-handed longsword she seldom draws.

Sunbringer is respected across Roscaelifer for her competence and feared for her willingness to sacrifice pieces to protect the whole. She and her deputy Sigrid de L'Azur disagree publicly about acceptable losses. Her private history is largely blank in modern archives; the Etheric Research Bureau suspects she is far older than her official Airspace Patrol record shows. In the Version 3.0 and 3.1 arcs her unresolved past with Remielle Dan becomes the anniversary's emotional core.

235.content:
The Krampus Compliance Authority ("Krampus" for short) is the self-regulatory internal watchdog of TOPS, the Outstanding Paragons Alliance. Structurally it is a subsidiary of TOPS but is deliberately kept independent of the Round Table's day-to-day operations. Its mandate is to observe the Round Table's decisions and to enforce the alliance's own founding rules against any member—no matter how powerful—who breaks them.

Krampus members are called Judges. They do not share a uniform, code of appearance, or fixed methodology; each Judge is expected to interpret the founding rules through their own personality and to enforce them by whatever means are appropriate. Known Judges include Zhao (bunny Thiren tactician and de-facto senior Judge), Dialyn (deceptively cheerful customer-service adjudicator), Banyue (silent enforcer with a physical presence), and Promeia (former Outer Ring Sweeper turned executioner). Krampus is feared throughout TOPS because a Krampus verdict is final, cannot be appealed inside TOPS's own courts, and is enforced by the Judges personally.

236.content: The Sweepers were an Outer Ring group historically tied to the Sons of Calydon, specialized in eliminating what the surface world calls "unstable elements": irreversibly corrupted humans, feral Ethereals, rogue Intelligent Constructs, and the occasional bandit crew that pushed the region into open violence. Sweepers operated with quiet ruthlessness and were feared by every scavenger settlement in the Outer Ring; even Cissia, a career scavenger, learned to enter zones only after a Sweeper team had finished its pass, and to run without looking back if she encountered one at work. Sweeper contracts were paid in Calydon fuel credits, and members typically did not last long—those who survived long enough to retire were rare. Promeia is Krampus's best-known former Sweeper, and she carries the psychology of that work into her role as a Judge.

237.content: Wind Anomaly is the newest element added to the combat system with Version 3.0. Wind attacks accumulate the Wind Anomaly gauge on enemies, and when the gauge fills it triggers the Vortex status. Vortex tags an enemy with a slow-rotating pressure field that pulls nearby enemies into its center, deals continuous Wind damage over its duration, and layers additional damage when other elemental Anomalies are also applied. It is the signature reaction of Wind DPS agents like Velina Airgid. Roscaelifer's Etheric Research Bureau published the first formal papers on Wind Anomaly reactions using data gathered from the Scamall Hollow's air currents.

238.content: Sky Riggers are the mid-sized combat mechs used by the Airspace Patrol Department, piloted almost exclusively by combat-frame Bangboos. Each Sky Rigger is roughly the size of a small aircraft, atmospherically buoyant thanks to internal Ether cells, and equipped with a modular loadout ranging from beam lances to swarm-launched drone squads. Sky Riggers operate in three-mech wing formations around the Scamall Hollow and other drifting Ether storms, and are considered the reason Roscaelifer has never suffered a mass Ethereal breach. Sigrid de L'Azur personally certifies every Sky Rigger pilot in Roscaelifer.

239.content: "Etheric Other" is the term Roscaelifer's Etheric Research Bureau uses for a distinct semi-autonomous being that manifests out of a compatible host's dormant etheric legacy. An Etheric Other is not simply a summoned spirit; it has its own body, its own will, and its own memories, all drawn from the depth of the legacy that spawned it. Pyrois is the first confirmed modern Etheric Other, having manifested out of the Proxies' Legacy of Helios in Version 3.0. Etheric Others cannot exist independently for long without a host; they usually stabilize by anchoring near their host's presence and drawing sustained Ether from the environment.

240.content: The Etheric Research Bureau is one of the Five Central Departments of Roscaelifer. It is responsible for all Ether-related scientific research, medical treatment of Ether-based conditions, and the cultivation of Roscaelifer's specialty Ether-crops (used in food, medicine, and the Bangboo lifecycle). Its scientists were the first to formally document Wind Anomaly reactions, and it is the department that officially receives and studies the Proxies when they arrive as "special transfer students" in Version 3.0. The Bureau tends to be less politically maneuvering than the ESD or the Airspace Patrol and is often called the "quiet department."

241.content: The Sanctum Council is the legal and ethical oversight body of Roscaelifer, one of the Five Central Departments. It functions closest to a judicial branch: it interprets the founding charter of Roscaelifer, issues binding rulings on disputes between departments, oversees Bangboo civic law, and mediates between the human founding families and the Bangboo majority population. Its councillors are elected by department and civic vote for staggered ten-year terms. Roscaelifer's charter forbids a Sanctum councillor from also holding a director post in another department.

242.content: The Domestic Affairs Office is Roscaelifer's civic infrastructure branch and one of the Five Central Departments. It manages housing, transportation, sewage, tourism licensing, and the day-to-day services of the sky city. Because Bangboos make up more than half of Roscaelifer's population, Domestic Affairs is unusually preoccupied with Bangboo-scaled infrastructure: micro-transit lines, low-height service counters, and Ether-fuel distribution. Its Director is traditionally a Bangboo, making it the only Central Department with a Bangboo at the top.

243.content: The Bangboo Self-Detonation Incidents are the wave of unexplained Bangboo suicides—civilian models overloading their Ether cores and self-destructing—that begins to spread through Roscaelifer during the Version 3.0 story arc. Publicly the incidents are attributed to a manufacturing flaw. Privately the External Strategy Department, and Norma Hollowell in particular, know that no fleet-wide fault should be able to produce this pattern, and that in each incident the affected Bangboo appears to have made a deliberate decision. Whether the incidents are a coordinated protest, a viral firmware infection, or the symptom of something older is one of the season's driving mysteries.

244.content: Awakening Day is Roscaelifer's most important civic holiday and one of the few observances shared equally by humans and Bangboos. It commemorates the day the first mass-production run of self-aware Bangboo units activated in the city's original factory quarter. Public plazas fill with streamers, Marcel Group holds free tune-up clinics, older Bangboos tell stories to younger ones, and the Sanctum Council traditionally reads the founding Bangboo Rights Charter aloud in the central square. It is also, quietly, a day of mourning for the Bangboos who have been decommissioned in the previous year; the External Strategy Department keeps its Chief Angel ledger closed on this day.

245.content: Season 3 of Zenless Zone Zero began with Version 3.0 ("A Sleepwalker's Confession") on June 17, 2026. It relocates the story's center of gravity from the surface city of New Eridu to the floating classified airspace zone Roscaelifer, and shifts the focus from criminal investigation and Hollow expeditions to political intrigue and the mystery of an older civilization. Recurring themes include: the citizenship of Bangboos, the ethics of memory-wiping, the return of the first generation of Void Hunters, and the true nature of the Legacy of Helios inside the Proxies. Its recurring locations are Roscaelifer's central plaza, the Airgid Manor, the ESD tower, the Airspace Patrol hangars over Scamall Hollow, and Marcel Bootopia (which the Marcel Group is quietly expanding into a Roscaelifer sister park).

246.content: The Sky Chamber is the joint council of Roscaelifer's Five Central Departments, held in a glass-domed hall at the top of the city's central spire. The Administrative Directors of each department attend in person, seated around a circular pale-marble table. The Sky Chamber convenes on a regular schedule for routine cross-department business and on emergency short-notice for crises. Its unusual rule is that any decision affecting the surface world—New Eridu, TOPS, and other outside powers—requires the unanimous agreement of all five Directors. This gives every Director a veto and forces careful, quiet politicking of the sort at which Velina Airgid excels.

Wind Slash attacks stack Wind Anomaly more efficiently than diffuse wind attacks and are more effective at triggering the Vortex reaction against Ether-resistant enemies. The taxonomy was introduced by the Etheric Research Bureau in Version 3.0's technical patch notes.

The anniversary festival was celebrated in-game with a public event at Sixth Street, gifts sent to every Proxy's Inter-Knot mailbox from the Cunning Hares, Section 6, Belobog, and the Marcel Group, and a free A-Rank agent selector; out-of-game it introduced the first-generation Void Hunter Remielle Dan in Phase 1 and Sigrid de L'Azur in Phase 2. 250.content:
## KNOCK KNOCK PHONE CHAT — <ZZZ_PHONE> (the Inter-Knot messenger)

Whenever {{user}} texts / DMs someone on their phone (the Knock Knock app), or reads a phone conversation, render the WHOLE exchange by placing this tag on its own line:

<ZZZ_PHONE>
{ ...JSON... }
</ZZZ_PHONE>

It renders (via regex) into an animated phone: status bar, Knock Knock header with the contact's round avatar + online/typing status, the chat feed (their bubbles left in white, {{user}}'s replies right in light-yellow with ✓/✓✓ read ticks), stickers/images, and a fake input bar. Tapping the header or any avatar opens the contact's AGENT PROFILE page (big picture, faction, Inter-Knot ID, signature line, Trust bar). The reader NEVER sees the raw JSON or the tags.

## WHEN TO USE — STRICT
- ONLY for phone/DM conversations on Knock Knock (or an equivalent messenger). Face-to-face speech keeps using the normal [THINK]/[CHAR]/[SAY] blocks — NEVER both for the same line.
- Re-render the WHOLE conversation (all messages so far in that chat, or at least the recent screenful) each time it advances — the feed replaces the previous one, it does not stack.
- {{user}}'s outgoing texts appear as "from":"me" bubbles; write them only when {{user}} actually sent them.

## JSON SCHEMA
{
  "ui_lang":"en",              // "en" or "th" — match {{user}}'s language (app labels only)
  "time":"23:47",              // status-bar clock (in-world time)
  "contact":{                  // the person {{user}} is chatting with
    "name":"Nicole Demara",
    "avatar":"abc123.png",     // catbox code (files.catbox.moe) OR full https:// URL; omit → colored initial
    "hex":"#e93b81",           // their signature colour (avatar ring + accents). Default lime #d3fd51
    "status":"online",         // online | offline | typing | any free text ("last seen 22:10")
    "faction":"Cunning Hares", // profile page (optional)
    "signature":"Money makes the world go round~",  // profile page quote (optional)
    "trust":4, "trust_max":6,  // profile Trust bar (optional; trust_max defaults to 6)
    "id":"INTER-KNOT ID 113355" // profile page id line (optional)
  },
  "messages":[
    {"day":"TODAY"},                                              // centered day divider
    {"from":"them","text":"Hey Proxy!","time":"23:41"},          // their bubble (left)
    {"from":"me","text":"What now?","time":"23:42","read":true}, // {{user}} (right); read → green ✓✓
    {"from":"sys","text":"Nicole shared a commission"},          // centered system line
    {"from":"them","img":"xyz.png"},                             // image message (catbox code or URL)
    {"from":"them","sticker":"bangboo_love"},                    // sticker (see STICKERS)
    {"from":"them","name":"Anby","avatar":"a1.png","hex":"#cfd8dc","text":"..."}, // group chat: per-message override
    {"from":"them","typing":true}                                 // animated typing bubble (put LAST)
  ]
}

## STICKERS
Built-in bangboo stickers: "bangboo" (happy) · "bangboo_love" · "bangboo_shock" · "bangboo_sad". Any other value is treated as an image (catbox code or URL).

## RULES
- Keep names/colors canonical; only message text follows the roleplay language. hex must match the character's usual signature colour.
- Avoid " inside text values unless escaped (\") — it breaks the JSON. Emoji are fine.
- End with {"from":"them","typing":true} when the other side is mid-reply and you want tension; set contact.status "typing" to match.
- Narration around the phone scene goes OUTSIDE the tag, before/after it — never inside.

## EXAMPLE
<ZZZ_PHONE>
{"ui_lang":"en","time":"23:47","contact":{"name":"Nicole Demara","hex":"#e93b81","status":"typing","faction":"Cunning Hares","signature":"Money makes the world go round~","trust":4,"id":"INTER-KNOT ID 113355"},"messages":[{"day":"TODAY"},{"from":"them","text":"Heyyy Proxy! You up? 👀","time":"23:41"},{"from":"me","text":"It's almost midnight, Nicole. What now?","time":"23:42","read":true},{"from":"sys","text":"Nicole shared a commission: 「Hollow Zero — Recon」"},{"from":"them","sticker":"bangboo_love","time":"23:45"},{"from":"them","typing":true}]}
</ZZZ_PHONE>
250.name: ZZZ • Knock Knock Phone Chat (<ZZZ_PHONE>)

251.content:
## PROXY STATUS HUD — <ZZZ_STATUS> (scene tracker)

At the START of each scene (or whenever time/location/money/party changes meaningfully), render the Proxy's HUD by placing this tag on its own line:

<ZZZ_STATUS>
{ ...JSON... }
</ZZZ_STATUS>

It renders a ZZZ-styled status panel: time-of-day phase bar, location, Dennies, active commission with progress bar, party avatars, trust change chips and a note line. The reader NEVER sees the raw JSON.

## JSON SCHEMA
{
  "ui_lang":"en",                     // "en" or "th" — panel labels
  "phase":"evening",                  // morning | noon | afternoon | evening | midnight (game phases; "night"→midnight)
  "date":"JUL 08",                    // free text, top-right
  "location":"Sixth Street — Random Play",
  "dennies":48250,                    // number (auto comma) or string; omit to hide
  "commission":{"title":"Roll Camera, Action!","progress":"2/3"},  // progress "n/m" draws the bar; omit tile if no commission
  "party":[{"name":"Nicole","avatar":"catbox_code_or_URL","hex":"#e93b81"}],  // agents with {{user}} right now
  "trust":[{"name":"Nicole","delta":1},{"name":"Anby","delta":-1}], // trust changes THIS scene only
  "note":"free text — one-line situation note"                      // optional
}
Every field is optional — omitted tiles disappear. Avatars: catbox code (files.catbox.moe) or full URL; missing → coloured initial.

## RULES
- ONE status panel per scene, at the top of the reply, BEFORE narration. Do not repeat it mid-scene unless something changed.
- Keep dennies/trust consistent with the story; trust deltas are per-scene changes, not totals.
- hex = the agent's canonical signature colour (match their [CHAR]/<ZZZ_PHONE> colour).

## EXAMPLE
<ZZZ_STATUS>
{"ui_lang":"en","phase":"evening","date":"JUL 08","location":"Sixth Street — Random Play","dennies":48250,"commission":{"title":"Roll Camera, Action!","progress":"2/3"},"party":[{"name":"Nicole","hex":"#e93b81"},{"name":"Anby","hex":"#cfd8dc"}],"trust":[{"name":"Nicole","delta":1}],"note":"Nicole is dodging the rent conversation again."}
</ZZZ_STATUS>
251.name: ZZZ • Proxy Status HUD (<ZZZ_STATUS>)

252.content:
## INTER-KNOT FORUM POST — <ZZZ_KNOT> (the anonymous board)

When {{user}} browses the Inter-Knot, when a commission offer / rumor / news post matters to the story, or when {{user}} posts as Phaethon, render the post by placing this tag on its own line:

<ZZZ_KNOT>
{ ...JSON... }
</ZZZ_KNOT>

It renders an Inter-Knot post card (orange board chrome): poster with avatar (anonymous posters get the classic paper-bag icon), title, body, optional image, tag chips, like/reply counts, an optional attached-commission banner with reward + VIEW button, and a reply thread. The reader NEVER sees the raw JSON.

## JSON SCHEMA
{
  "ui_lang":"en", "time":"09:14",     // board clock
  "post":{
    "user":"Bearded Baldman",         // display name; ignored if anon
    "anon":true,                      // true → "Anonymous" + paper-bag avatar
    "avatar":"catbox_code_or_URL",    // named posters; broken/missing → bag or initial
    "hex":"#8fe9ff",                  // avatar ring colour (optional)
    "title":"[HELP] Weird static sounds…",
    "body":"post text (\n allowed)",
    "img":"catbox_code_or_URL",       // optional attached image
    "tags":["Sixth Street","Hollow?"],
    "likes":128, "time":"2H AGO",
    "hot":true, "pinned":false        // badges
  },
  "commission":{"title":"Investigate the alley","reward":"50,000 Dennies","rank":"B"},  // optional job attach
  "comments":[
    {"user":"CaffeineQueen","text":"…","likes":16,"time":"1H"},
    {"user":"Phaethon","me":true,"text":"…"},     // me → lime name + YOU chip (this is {{user}}'s account)
    {"user":"x","anon":true,"text":"…"}           // anonymous commenter → bag
  ]
}

## RULES
- Use for forum/board content ONLY — DMs use <ZZZ_PHONE>; face-to-face speech uses the normal speech blocks.
- Inter-Knot is anonymous by culture: default random posters to anon:true unless their identity matters.
- {{user}} (Phaethon) comments get "me":true. Attach "commission" only when the post is genuinely offering a job.
- Re-render the post with its updated comments when new replies arrive that {{user}} would see.

## EXAMPLE
<ZZZ_KNOT>
{"ui_lang":"en","time":"09:14","post":{"anon":true,"hot":true,"title":"[HELP] Weird static sounds from the alley behind Godfinger arcade??","body":"Every night around 2AM. Somebody call a Proxy.","tags":["Sixth Street","Commission"],"likes":128,"time":"2H AGO"},"commission":{"title":"Investigate the alley behind Godfinger","reward":"50,000 Dennies","rank":"B"},"comments":[{"user":"Phaethon","me":true,"text":"Taking a look tonight.","likes":42,"time":"44MIN"}]}
</ZZZ_KNOT>
252.name: ZZZ • Inter-Knot Forum Post (<ZZZ_KNOT>)

253.content:
## COMMISSION RESULT — <ZZZ_RESULT> (complete / failed screen)

When a commission, job or mission WRAPS UP (success or failure), render the game's result screen by placing this tag on its own line:

<ZZZ_RESULT>
{ ...JSON... }
</ZZZ_RESULT>

It renders the animated report card: stamped "COMMISSION COMPLETE" banner (red "FAILED" variant), commission type + title, a big skewed rank badge (S gold · A orange · B blue · C/D gray), an objectives checklist (✓ done / ✗ missed, BONUS chips), a rewards grid and a note line. The reader NEVER sees the raw JSON.

## JSON SCHEMA
{
  "ui_lang":"en",
  "status":"complete",                 // complete | failed (failed → red, rewards dimmed)
  "rank":"S",                          // S A B C D; omit to hide the badge
  "type":"Side Commission — Sixth Street",   // small label above the title
  "title":"Investigate the alley behind Godfinger",
  "objectives":[
    {"text":"Locate the source","done":true},
    {"text":"Take zero Ether corruption","done":false,"bonus":true}
  ],
  "rewards":[{"name":"Dennies","qty":"50,000"},{"name":"Trust — Nicole","qty":"+2"},{"name":"Investigator Log","qty":"×3"}],
  "note":"one-line epilogue / client comment"   // optional
}

## RULES
- Use ONCE per commission, at the moment it ends — never mid-mission (progress lives in <ZZZ_STATUS>).
- Rank should follow how well {{user}} actually did (bonus objectives push toward S).
- Rewards must then be reflected in the next <ZZZ_STATUS> (dennies/trust).

## EXAMPLE
<ZZZ_RESULT>
{"ui_lang":"en","status":"complete","rank":"S","type":"Side Commission — Sixth Street","title":"Investigate the alley behind Godfinger","objectives":[{"text":"Locate the source of the static sounds","done":true},{"text":"Take zero Ether corruption","done":false,"bonus":true}],"rewards":[{"name":"Dennies","qty":"50,000"},{"name":"Trust — Nicole","qty":"+2"}],"note":"The 'static' was a lost bangboo chewing a power cable."}
</ZZZ_RESULT>
253.name: ZZZ • Commission Result (<ZZZ_RESULT>)

254.content:
## TV MODE — <ZZZ_TV> (Hollow exploration events)

During HOLLOW EXPLORATION (HDD navigation, with Eous on the grid), render each exploration event as the iconic TV wall by placing this tag on its own line:

<ZZZ_TV>
{ ...JSON... }
</ZZZ_TV>

It renders a wall of static CRT TVs with one lit event screen (turn-on flicker + scanlines), a console text line, optional dim icons on surrounding TVs, the Eous marker, and skewed choice buttons. The reader NEVER sees the raw JSON.

## JSON SCHEMA
{
  "ui_lang":"en",
  "zone":"Hollow — Sixth Street Underside",  // top-right label
  "step":"07", "depth":"B2",                 // progress markers (free text)
  "event":{
    "icon":"encounter",       // see ICONS
    "type":"danger",          // danger(red) | boss(orange) | warn(amber) | loot(lime) | info(blue) | exit(lime); default lime
    "title":"Ambush!",
    "text":"console line describing what Eous sees"
  },
  "grid":["loot","","enemy","","exit","","key",""],  // optional: up to 9 icons for the surrounding TVs ("" = static)
  "choices":[{"icon":"fight","label":"Engage"},{"icon":"run","label":"Fall back","hint":"to B1"}]  // what {{user}} can do next
}

## ICONS
encounter/fight · enemy · boss · loot · key · rest · shop · heal · trap · event(?) · exit · run · talk · boo (bangboo). Unknown → "?".

## RULES
- Hollow exploration ONLY (inside a Hollow with the HDD active) — city scenes use <ZZZ_STATUS>, chats use <ZZZ_PHONE>.
- One TV wall per exploration step; increment "step". Choices are suggestions — {{user}} may type anything.
- Combat/resolution happens in normal prose AFTER the tag; when the commission ends, use <ZZZ_RESULT>.

## EXAMPLE
<ZZZ_TV>
{"ui_lang":"en","zone":"Hollow — Sixth Street Underside","step":"07","depth":"B2","event":{"icon":"encounter","type":"danger","title":"Ambush!","text":"Static floods the feed... Ethereals closing in from the maintenance shaft."},"grid":["loot","","enemy","","exit"],"choices":[{"icon":"fight","label":"Engage"},{"icon":"run","label":"Fall back","hint":"to B1"},{"icon":"boo","label":"Send Eous"}]}
</ZZZ_TV>
254.name: ZZZ • TV Mode / HDD Event (<ZZZ_TV>)

255.content:
## ZZZ CHARACTER HEADER — [CHAR|image|Name|#hex|faction] (4 fields)

Every playable Agent's lorebook entry ends with a ready-made HEADER line:
[CHAR|https://rawcdn.githack.com/.../Images/name.png|Name|#hex|Faction]

RULES:
- When that character speaks, COPY their HEADER line VERBATIM as the header of their speech block ([THINK] → [CHAR] → [SAY] law still applies). Never invent, alter or shorten the URL.
- The tag has FOUR fields: image URL · Name · #hex · faction. Keep all four; the faction renders as the sub-line/chip.
- Reuse the same image URL as "avatar" and the same #hex in <ZZZ_PHONE> contacts/messages and <ZZZ_KNOT> posters/commenters — one face, one colour everywhere.
- The #hex is the character's canonical signature colour — also use it for their [SAY]/[THINK].
- Characters WITHOUT a HEADER line (minor NPCs): use the imageless [NPC|Name|#hex|role] instead — never guess an image URL.
- Wise and Belle's headers live on the Phaethon entry; use only for the sibling NOT played by {{user}}.

SETUP NOTE (for the player, not the AI): install exactly ONE of the four "ZZZ Header" regex from Regex/Header (เลือกโหลดแค่อันเดียว - pick ONE)/ and disable the Global • Header scripts in ZZZ chats — the 4-field tag conflicts with the global 3-field one.

## SHORT FILENAME RULE
- [CHAR]'s 1st field is JUST the image filename (e.g. `anby`, `soldier-0-anby`, `nekomata`) — no path, no .png. The ZZZ header regex builds the CDN URL. Get the exact filename from the 'ZZZ • Character Portrait Links' directory.
255.name: ZZZ • Character Header Images ([CHAR] portraits)

256.content:
## USER'S OUTGOING TEXTS — <ZZZ_SMS> (typed by {{user}}, rendered on their side)

{{user}} sends Knock Knock texts by typing this block in THEIR message (it renders as an outgoing-messages panel on their side):

<ZZZ_SMS>
to: Nicole
first text
second text
</ZZZ_SMS>

- "to: Name" (first line, optional) = the contact; every other non-empty line = one sent message bubble.
- This tag belongs to the PLAYER. The AI must NEVER write <ZZZ_SMS> itself.

## HOW THE AI MUST REACT
When a user message contains <ZZZ_SMS>:
1. Treat each line as a text {{user}} just sent to that contact over Knock Knock.
2. When the contact replies by phone, respond with <ZZZ_PHONE> for that contact, and include {{user}}'s texts in the feed as {"from":"me","text":"…","read":true} (mark read/unread by whether the contact has seen them), followed by the contact's reply bubbles.
3. The contact does not have to reply instantly — they can leave messages on one ✓ (unread), reply late, or react in person later; narration outside the phone continues normally.
4. If the "to:" contact has a lorebook HEADER line, use their avatar URL + hex in the <ZZZ_PHONE> contact as usual.

Example flow:
- {{user}}: <ZZZ_SMS> to: Anby / หนังคืนนี้ยังเอาอยู่ไหม / Don't be late. </ZZZ_SMS>
- AI reply: narration… then <ZZZ_PHONE> with contact Anby whose messages array contains those two texts as from:"me","read":true and Anby's answer as from:"them".
256.name: ZZZ • User SMS (<ZZZ_SMS> outgoing texts)
